title: Usher (entertainer)

infobox.value: [Tameka Foster](/wiki/Tameka_Foster "Tameka Foster")  ​  ​ (m. 2007; div. 2009)​      Grace Miguel  ​  ​ (m. 2015; div. 2018)​      Jennifer Goicoechea  ​  ​ (m. 2024)​
[Full list](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Usher "List of awards and nominations received by Usher")

* [LaFace](/wiki/LaFace_Records "LaFace Records") * [Arista](/wiki/Arista_Records "Arista Records") * [Jive](/wiki/Jive_Records "Jive Records") * [RCA](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records") * [Legacy](/wiki/Legacy_Recordings "Legacy Recordings") * US * [RBMG](/wiki/RBMG_Records "RBMG Records") * Mega * [Gamma](/wiki/Gamma_(media_company) "Gamma (media company)")

(born October 14, 1978) is an American singer, songwriter, dancer, and actor. He is recognized as an influential figure in [contemporary R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") and [pop music](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music"). In 1994, Usher released his [self-titled debut album](/wiki/Usher_(album) "Usher (album)") at the age of 15. He rose to fame with the release of his second album, *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_album) "My Way (Usher album)")* (1997), which spawned his first US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") number-one single "[Nice & Slow](/wiki/Nice_%26_Slow "Nice & Slow")", and the top-two singles: the [title track](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_song) "My Way (Usher song)") and "[You Make Me Wanna...](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")".

**Usher Raymond IV** (born October 14, 1978) is an American singer, songwriter, dancer, and actor. He is recognized as an influential figure in [contemporary R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") and [pop music](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music"). In 1994, Usher released his [self-titled debut album](/wiki/Usher_(album) "Usher (album)") at the age of 15. He rose to fame with the release of his second album, *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_album) "My Way (Usher album)")* (1997), which spawned his first US [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") number-one single "[Nice & Slow](/wiki/Nice_%26_Slow "Nice & Slow")", and the top-two singles: the [title track](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_song) "My Way (Usher song)") and "[You Make Me Wanna...](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")".

Usher's third album, *[8701](/wiki/8701 "8701")* (2001), saw continued success, selling eight million copies and yielding the US number-one singles, "[U Remind Me](/wiki/U_Remind_Me "U Remind Me")" and "[U Got It Bad](/wiki/U_Got_It_Bad "U Got It Bad")". His fourth album, *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* (2004), sold over [one million copies first-week](/wiki/List_of_fastest-selling_albums#United_States "List of fastest-selling albums") and was supported by four consecutive US number-one singles: "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")", "[Burn](/wiki/Burn_(Usher_song) "Burn (Usher song)")", "[Confessions Part II](/wiki/Confessions_Part_II "Confessions Part II")", and "[My Boo](/wiki/My_Boo_(Usher_and_Alicia_Keys_song) "My Boo (Usher and Alicia Keys song)")". The album is the [best-selling album by a Black artist of the 21st century](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_albums_of_the_21st_century "List of best-selling albums of the 21st century") and received a diamond certification from the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA). After splitting from his manager and mother [Jonnetta Patton](/wiki/Jonnetta_Patton "Jonnetta Patton") in 2007, he released the album *[Here I Stand](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album) "Here I Stand (Usher album)")* (2008), which spawned the number-one single "[Love in This Club](/wiki/Love_in_This_Club "Love in This Club")". *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* named him the second [most successful artist of the decade](/wiki/Billboard_Decade-End#2000s "Billboard Decade-End"), the number-one Hot 100 artist of the decade, and ranked *Confessions* as the top solo album of the decade.

Usher's sixth album, *[Raymond v. Raymond](/wiki/Raymond_v._Raymond "Raymond v. Raymond")* (2010), contained his ninth number-one single, "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")", making him one of the few artists to top the *Billboard* Hot 100 in three consecutive decades. His debut [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play"), *[Versus](/wiki/Versus_(Usher_EP) "Versus (Usher EP)")* (2010), produced the top-five single "[DJ Got Us Fallin' in Love](/wiki/DJ_Got_Us_Fallin%27_in_Love "DJ Got Us Fallin' in Love")". Usher's seventh album, *[Looking 4 Myself](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself "Looking 4 Myself")* (2012) saw further [electronic](/wiki/Electronic_music "Electronic music") production and spawned the top-ten single "[Scream](/wiki/Scream_(Usher_song) "Scream (Usher song)")". He followed it with the albums *[Hard II Love](/wiki/Hard_II_Love "Hard II Love")* (2016) and *[Coming Home](/wiki/Coming_Home_(Usher_album) "Coming Home (Usher album)")* (2024). His [Super Bowl LVIII halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LVIII_halftime_show "Super Bowl LVIII halftime show") in 2024 drew 123 million viewers within the US.

Usher has sold over 100 million records worldwide, making him one of the [best-selling music artists of all time](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_music_artists "List of best-selling music artists").[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7) *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* ranked him among the [Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century](/wiki/Greatest_Pop_Stars_of_the_21st_Century "Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century"). His [accolades](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Usher "List of awards and nominations received by Usher") include 8 [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award"), 12 [Soul Train Music Awards](/wiki/Soul_Train_Music_Awards "Soul Train Music Awards"), 8 [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Award "American Music Award"), 18 [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award"), 7 [BET Awards](/wiki/BET_Awards "BET Awards") (including the [BET Lifetime Achievement Award](/wiki/BET_Lifetime_Achievement_Award "BET Lifetime Achievement Award")) and a star on the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame"). He was inducted into the [Black Music & Entertainment Walk of Fame](/wiki/Black_Music_%26_Entertainment_Walk_of_Fame "Black Music & Entertainment Walk of Fame") in 2021. In 2008, Usher co-founded the record label [Raymond-Braun Media Group (RBMG)](/wiki/RBMG_Records "RBMG Records") with [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun") to manage the career of singer [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"). Outside of music, he founded the charitable non-profit [Usher's New Look](/wiki/Usher%27s_New_Look "Usher's New Look") in 1999, served as a coach on *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_TV_series) "The Voice (American TV series)")* (2013–2014), and starred in films such as *[The Faculty](/wiki/The_Faculty "The Faculty")* (1998), *[She's All That](/wiki/She%27s_All_That "She's All That")* (1999), *[Light It Up](/wiki/Light_It_Up_(film) "Light It Up (film)")* (1999), *[In the Mix](/wiki/In_the_Mix_(film) "In the Mix (film)")* (2005) and *[Hands of Stone](/wiki/Hands_of_Stone "Hands of Stone")* (2016).

## Early life

Usher Raymond IV was born on October 14, 1978, in [Dallas](/wiki/Dallas "Dallas"), [Texas](/wiki/Texas "Texas").[[8]](#cite_note-swe-8) His parents are [Jonnetta Patton](/wiki/Jonnetta_Patton "Jonnetta Patton") ([née](/wiki/N%C3%A9e "Née") O'Neal) and Usher Raymond III.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) He spent the majority of his young life in [Chattanooga, Tennessee](/wiki/Chattanooga,_Tennessee "Chattanooga, Tennessee"). His father left the family when Usher was a year old.[[11]](#cite_note-ReidKing-11) Shortly before dying in January 2008, Usher III reconciled with him and apologized for being absent. The singer once called this period "the most valuable time" they shared together. Usher IV has a younger half-brother, James Lackey, from Jonnetta's marriage to Terry Patton, and grew up with the three of them.[[12]](#cite_note-AboutParents-12) Directed by his mother, he joined the local church youth choir in Chattanooga when he was nine years old. There, his grandmother discovered his ability to sing, although it was not until Usher joined a singing group that she considered he could sing professionally.[[11]](#cite_note-ReidKing-11) Believing that a bigger city would provide greater opportunities for showcasing him, Usher's family moved to [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta"), [Georgia](/wiki/Georgia_(U.S._state) "Georgia (U.S. state)"), where there was a more conducive environment for beginning singers.[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13) While in Atlanta, he attended [North Springs High School](/wiki/North_Springs_Charter_School_of_Arts_and_Sciences "North Springs Charter School of Arts and Sciences").[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Career

### 1991–1996: NuBeginning and *Usher*

At age 12, Usher became one of five members of a local R&B group called NuBeginning, and recorded ten songs with them in 1991. The quintet was organized by local music promoter Darryl Wheeler, and his groupmates were Anthony Byrd, Adrian Johnson, Reginald McKibbon, and Charles Yarbrough. These were included on their self-titled album, which was released during 1993. It spawned a single titled "A Mother's Luv". *NuBeginning* at first was only made available regionally and by [mail order](/wiki/Mail_order "Mail order") before being re-released nationally in April 2002 as *NuBeginning Featuring Usher Raymond IV* by Hip-O Records.[[15]](#cite_note-D'AngeloNu-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) This ended up being the group's only album.[[18]](#cite_note-18) Patton felt her son was better off as a solo artist and pulled him out at age 13, later calling his time with the group "a bad experience".[[11]](#cite_note-ReidKing-11) He initially worried this would end his musical dreams and did not yet understand the choice before she stated "your world is only beginning" and planned out future success without the group.[[12]](#cite_note-AboutParents-12)

At age 13, Usher met A.J. Alexander at a local talent show in Atlanta.[[19]](#cite_note-19) Alexander, who at the time was [Bobby Brown](/wiki/Bobby_Brown "Bobby Brown")'s bodyguard, would take Usher around and have him perform in parking lots and talent shows.[[20]](#cite_note-20) Alexander invited Bryant Reid, an [A&R](/wiki/Artists_and_repertoire "Artists and repertoire") representative from [LaFace Records](/wiki/LaFace_Records "LaFace Records") to see Usher perform on the television talent show *[Star Search](/wiki/Star_Search "Star Search")*. Following the performance, he arranged an audition for Usher with [L.A. Reid](/wiki/L.A._Reid "L.A. Reid"), the co-founder of LaFace; Reid signed Usher on the spot to a contract with the record company after Usher sang the [Boyz II Men](/wiki/Boyz_II_Men "Boyz II Men") track "[End of the Road](/wiki/End_of_the_Road_(Boyz_II_Men_song) "End of the Road (Boyz II Men song)")".[[21]](#cite_note-AllmusicBio-21)[[22]](#cite_note-BillUsher-22) Patton left her job as a medical technician to manage Usher. His first solo song, "[Call Me a Mack](/wiki/Call_Me_a_Mack "Call Me a Mack")", was recorded for the soundtrack album to the 1993 [drama](/wiki/Drama_film "Drama film")-[romance film](/wiki/Romance_film "Romance film") *[Poetic Justice](/wiki/Poetic_Justice_(film) "Poetic Justice (film)")*.[[15]](#cite_note-D'AngeloNu-15)

While preparing for his solo debut album, Usher lost his voice. He was going through puberty and had a difficult time adjusting his vocals. L.A. Reid became skeptical of Usher, put his album on hold and considered dropping him from the label.[[23]](#cite_note-23) Usher pleaded with the label to keep him and they did. From this point Reid did not know what to do with Usher so he sent him to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") in the spring of 1994 to live with record executive [Sean Combs](/wiki/Sean_Combs "Sean Combs") to "attend" what Reid called "Flavor Camp". Usher quickly adapted to the lavish lifestyle of Combs although in an interview with *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*, he described this period as the "hardest days" of his life. "I had to knuckle up, figure shit out in New York" he said.[[24]](#cite_note-24)

On August 30, 1994, LaFace released Usher's [self-titled debut album](/wiki/Usher_(album) "Usher (album)") behind the co-executive production of Sean Combs.[[21]](#cite_note-AllmusicBio-21) *Usher* peaked at number twenty-five on the *Billboard* [Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums](/wiki/Top_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Albums "Top R&B/Hip-Hop Albums") chart and was accompanied by three singles: "[Can U Get wit It](/wiki/Can_U_Get_wit_It "Can U Get wit It")", "[Think of You](/wiki/Think_of_You_(Usher_song) "Think of You (Usher song)")", and "[The Many Ways](/wiki/The_Many_Ways "The Many Ways")".[[25]](#cite_note-25) The album sold over 500,000 copies, by December 2008.[[26]](#cite_note-PeopleBio-26) It received backlash because he was singing about sex, and him only being 15 years old, the public had a difficult time believing that this was so. LaFace had reservations again about signing Usher because the album was not as successful as they anticipated. At this time Usher's mother took control of his career and put him in more talent shows the summer of 1995, building him a larger fan base and ultimately giving L.A. Reid more confidence in Usher.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

After graduating from high school, Usher continued to develop his skills as a stage performer and laid the groundwork for his second album. He also appeared on LaFace's version of "Let's Straighten It Out", a 1995 duet with fellow Atlanta teen recording artist [Monica](/wiki/Monica_(entertainer) "Monica (entertainer)");[[27]](#cite_note-27) and on "Dreamin'", from LaFace's [1996 Olympic Games](/wiki/1996_Olympic_Games "1996 Olympic Games") benefit album *Rhythm of the Games*. He was also featured on "I Swear I'm In Love" off the soundtrack to the 1996 film *[Kazaam](/wiki/Kazaam "Kazaam")*.[[28]](#cite_note-28)

### 1997–2003: *My Way* and *8701*

Usher developed a friendship with American record producer [Jermaine Dupri](/wiki/Jermaine_Dupri "Jermaine Dupri"), with whom he co-wrote and produced several tracks for his second album, *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_album) "My Way (Usher album)")*, released on September 16, 1997. The album's lead single, "[You Make Me Wanna...](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")", reached number one in the United Kingdom, becoming Usher's first record to be top single; this resulted in him increasing in popularity.[[29]](#cite_note-29)[[30]](#cite_note-30) It also became Usher's first gold- and platinum-certified single in the United States.[[31]](#cite_note-RIAA1-31) The album's second single, "[Nice & Slow](/wiki/Nice_%26_Slow "Nice & Slow")", peaked in January 1998 at number one on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), giving Usher his first US number-one single.[[32]](#cite_note-BillboardChart-32) In February of the same year, the single was certified platinum by the [Recording Industry Association of America](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_America "Recording Industry Association of America") (RIAA). *My Way* has been certified seven-time platinum in the United States.[[31]](#cite_note-RIAA1-31)[[33]](#cite_note-Certifications-33)

"[You Make Me Wanna...](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")" won the Best Male R&B/Soul Single at the [1999 Soul Train Music Awards](/wiki/1999_Soul_Train_Music_Awards "1999 Soul Train Music Awards").[[34]](#cite_note-34) In the closing months of 1997, Usher embarked on a series of tour engagements including a spot on Puffy's *No Way Out* tour, dates with [Mary J. Blige](/wiki/Mary_J._Blige "Mary J. Blige"), and the opening spot on [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson")'s *[The Velvet Rope Tour](/wiki/The_Velvet_Rope_Tour "The Velvet Rope Tour")*. Usher's first concert album, *[Live](/wiki/Live_(Usher_album) "Live (Usher album)")*, was released in 1999, which featured appearances by [Lil' Kim](/wiki/Lil%27_Kim "Lil' Kim"), [Jagged Edge](/wiki/Jagged_Edge_(American_group) "Jagged Edge (American group)"), [Trey Lorenz](/wiki/Trey_Lorenz "Trey Lorenz"), [Shanice](/wiki/Shanice "Shanice"), [Twista](/wiki/Twista "Twista") and [Manuel Seal](/wiki/Manuel_Seal "Manuel Seal"); the album has been certified gold in the United States.[[31]](#cite_note-RIAA1-31)

Usher made his acting debut on the [UPN](/wiki/UPN "UPN") television series *[Moesha](/wiki/Moesha "Moesha")*, which resulted in a recurring role on the series and subsequently his first film role in 1998's *[The Faculty](/wiki/The_Faculty "The Faculty")*.[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13)[[26]](#cite_note-PeopleBio-26) Usher's extracurricular activities outside of the recording industry gathered momentum over the following year, as he was cast in the soap opera *[The Bold and the Beautiful](/wiki/The_Bold_and_the_Beautiful "The Bold and the Beautiful")*.[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13)[[26]](#cite_note-PeopleBio-26) He completed two more films, *[She's All That](/wiki/She%27s_All_That "She's All That")*, and his first starring role in *[Light It Up](/wiki/Light_It_Up_(film) "Light It Up (film)")*.[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13)[[26]](#cite_note-PeopleBio-26) He also appeared in the [Disney](/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") TV movie *Geppetto*.[[35]](#cite_note-35)

Usher's third studio album, originally titled *All About U*, was stated to be released in early 2001.[[36]](#cite_note-36) The first single, "[Pop Ya Collar](/wiki/Pop_Ya_Collar "Pop Ya Collar")", was released in late 2000 and became a number two hit in the UK but underperformed in the United States.[[37]](#cite_note-AllmusicDiscog-37) The album was subsequently pushed back and retooled after select tracks were later leaked to the radio and Internet. At the same time, LaFace went dormant, moving most of its artists, including Usher, to its parent company, Arista Records.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] After having revised and renamed to *8701*, the album was released on August 7, 2001 (8.7.01).[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13) The first two singles "[U Remind Me](/wiki/U_Remind_Me "U Remind Me")" and "[U Got It Bad](/wiki/U_Got_It_Bad "U Got It Bad")" each topped the *Billboard* Hot 100 for four and six weeks, respectively. *8701* has been certified five-time platinum in the United States.[[31]](#cite_note-RIAA1-31)

Usher appeared in the 2001 film *[Texas Rangers](/wiki/Texas_Rangers_(film) "Texas Rangers (film)")*.[[38]](#cite_note-38) In February 2002, Usher won a Grammy for [Best Male R&B Vocal Performance](/wiki/Best_Male_R%26B_Vocal_Performance "Best Male R&B Vocal Performance") for "U Remind Me".[[39]](#cite_note-grammy-39) The next year, he won the same award for "[U Don't Have to Call](/wiki/U_Don%27t_Have_to_Call "U Don't Have to Call")",[[13]](#cite_note-Driven-13) making Usher the only artist aside from [Luther Vandross](/wiki/Luther_Vandross "Luther Vandross") and [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder") to win this award consecutively. In the summer of 2002, Usher contributed vocals to Combs' "[I Need a Girl (Part One)](/wiki/I_Need_a_Girl_(Part_One) "I Need a Girl (Part One)")". The year closed out with a trio of TV series appearances, all in November, on *[The Twilight Zone](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone "The Twilight Zone")*, *7th Heaven*, *Moesha*, and *American Dreams*, the latter in which Usher portrayed [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye").[[40]](#cite_note-40)[[41]](#cite_note-41)

### 2004–2009: *Confessions* and *Here I Stand*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Usher04.jpg/120px-Usher04.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher04.jpg)

Usher arriving in [Miami](/wiki/Miami "Miami"), Florida, to attend the [2004 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2004_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2004 MTV Video Music Awards")

Usher's fourth studio album, *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")*, was released on March 23, 2004—just as its first single, "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")", was in its sixth week at number one on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") and fifth week on top of the Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Singles Chart.[[42]](#cite_note-E!_-_Usher-42) The album's nearly 1.1 million unit debut sales was the highest first-week numbers ever scanned by a male R&B artist and the seventh best of the [Nielsen SoundScan](/wiki/Nielsen_SoundScan "Nielsen SoundScan") history.[[43]](#cite_note-Todd-43)[[44]](#cite_note-D'Angelo-44) The album has since sold over 20 million copies worldwide,[[45]](#cite_note-fifteen-45) with over 14 million in the United States, earning the album a Diamond certification from the RIAA.[[46]](#cite_note-46)[[47]](#cite_note-47)[[33]](#cite_note-Certifications-33)

The album's second and third singles, "[Burn](/wiki/Burn_(Usher_song) "Burn (Usher song)")" and "[Confessions Part II](/wiki/Confessions_Part_II "Confessions Part II")", also topped the *Billboard* Hot 100, the former for eight weeks. Usher became the first artist to top the *Billboard* [Hot 100 Airplay](/wiki/Hot_100_Airplay "Hot 100 Airplay") with four consecutive number-one singles,[[48]](#cite_note-48) In September 2004, "My Boo", a duet with American singer-songwriter [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys"), also peaked at number one at the *Billboard* Hot 100, becoming the album's fourth number-one single.[[49]](#cite_note-49) In December, the album's final single "[Caught Up](/wiki/Caught_Up_(Usher_song) "Caught Up (Usher song)")" peaked at number eight on the Hot 100.[[37]](#cite_note-AllmusicDiscog-37) By that time, Arista went inactive[[50]](#cite_note-50)[[51]](#cite_note-51) and following its parent, [Bertelsmann Music Group](/wiki/Bertelsmann_Music_Group "Bertelsmann Music Group") (BMG)'s merger with [Sony Music](/wiki/Sony_Music "Sony Music"),[[52]](#cite_note-52) his previous label, LaFace, was revived under the [Jive](/wiki/Jive_Records "Jive Records")/[Zomba Group](/wiki/Zomba_Group "Zomba Group") banner, bringing Usher back to the fold.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

*Confessions* earned Usher numerous awards, including four [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Award "American Music Award"), two [MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "MTV Europe Music Awards"), two [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2004_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2004 MTV Video Music Awards"), and three World Music Awards.[[53]](#cite_note-53)[[54]](#cite_note-54)[[55]](#cite_note-55)[[56]](#cite_note-56) At the 47th annual Grammy Awards ceremony in 2005, Usher won three awards, including: R&B Performance by a Duo or Group With Vocals for "My Boo", which he shared with Keys; Rap/Sung Collaboration for "Yeah!"; and Contemporary R&B Album for *Confessions*. At the 2004 [*Billboard* Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Awards "Billboard Music Awards"), Usher was recognized Artist of the Year, in addition to receiving 10 other accolades.[[57]](#cite_note-57)

Usher supported *Confessions* with "The Truth Tour". The tour set featured a small stage up on top of the main stage, where the band played with Usher and his supporting dancers left with enough room to perform. The smaller stage had a mini platform attached to it which lowered to the main stage and had two big staircases on both sides of it. To the left, a group of circular staircases climbed to the top, and to the right, there was a fire escape replete with steps and an elevator. [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") and [Christina Milian](/wiki/Christina_Milian "Christina Milian") were the opening acts for the tour.[[58]](#cite_note-58)

Usher at select dates would bring out a special guest, during his Atlanta show taping he brought out his artist at the time [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love") to rap a verse on the song "Throwback". During a more intimate part of the show, Usher's dancers selected singer [Monica](/wiki/Monica_(singer) "Monica (singer)") from the crowd to be serenaded by Usher while he sang album favorites "Do it to Me" and "Superstar". Usher also performed the "Confessions Part II" remix, where [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West") would appear from back of the stage. Then lastly Usher would also bring out artist [Lil Jon](/wiki/Lil_Jon "Lil Jon") and [Ludacris](/wiki/Ludacris "Ludacris") where they would finish the show performing the hit "[Yeah](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")". This concert date was filmed to make the official tour DVD of Truth Tour: Behind the Truth "Live from Atlanta". It has been certified by the RIAA selling more than 7 Million copies.[[59]](#cite_note-59)

In the spring of 2005, Usher scored a number three Hot 100 hit as a featured vocalist on [Lil Jon](/wiki/Lil_Jon "Lil Jon")'s "[Lovers & Friends](/wiki/Lovers_%26_Friends "Lovers & Friends")".[[60]](#cite_note-60) In 2007, Usher also collaborated with [R. Kelly](/wiki/R._Kelly "R. Kelly") on the track "[Same Girl](/wiki/Same_Girl_(R._Kelly_and_Usher_song) "Same Girl (R. Kelly and Usher song)")", for Kelly's album [Double Up](/wiki/Double_Up_(R._Kelly_album) "Double Up (R. Kelly album)").[[61]](#cite_note-61) He was also featured in a remix version of [Omarion](/wiki/Omarion "Omarion")'s "[Ice Box](/wiki/Ice_Box_(song) "Ice Box (song)")".[[62]](#cite_note-62) Usher also appeared on the track "Shake Down" on American singer-songwriter [Mary J. Blige](/wiki/Mary_J._Blige "Mary J. Blige")'s 2007 album *[Growing Pains](/wiki/Growing_Pains "Growing Pains")*.[[63]](#cite_note-63) According to [Robert Hilburn](/wiki/Robert_Hilburn "Robert Hilburn") of *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")*, Usher was voted number-one on the executive voting based "Pop's Power List" and described as a "sure-fire property".[[64]](#cite_note-64) In November 2005, Usher starred as a disc jockey named Darrell in the [Lions Gate](/wiki/Lions_Gate_Entertainment "Lions Gate Entertainment") film *[In the Mix](/wiki/In_the_Mix_(film) "In the Mix (film)")*.[[65]](#cite_note-65)

On August 22, 2006, Usher took over the role of Billy Flynn in the long-running Broadway revival of the musical *[Chicago](/wiki/Chicago_(musical) "Chicago (musical)")*.[[66]](#cite_note-66) Usher's opening night brought out stars [Penélope Cruz](/wiki/Pen%C3%A9lope_Cruz "Penélope Cruz"), [Gabrielle Union](/wiki/Gabrielle_Union "Gabrielle Union"), [Clive Davis](/wiki/Clive_Davis "Clive Davis"), and [Rosie Perez](/wiki/Rosie_Perez "Rosie Perez") among others.[[67]](#cite_note-67) Usher played the role of Flynn for two months but a case of strep throat brought an abrupt ending to Usher's run in *Chicago,* which saw a spike in ticket sales after he joined the cast. The *New York Post* reports that Usher's absence cost the production an estimated $400,000 in refunds to disappointed ticket buyers. The producer of Chicago, [Barry Weissler](/wiki/Barry_Weissler "Barry Weissler"), issued a statement sending his "thoughts and best wishes to Usher for a quick and speedy recovery. Usher made a spectacular Broadway debut, bringing a great dedication, work ethic and his amazing talent to the show. We all hope that he might return at some point for the many fans that were unable to see his wonderful performance as Billy Flynn. Usher has found a new home on Broadway and is welcomed back anytime."[[68]](#cite_note-68)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/08/Usher_Looking_To_Obama.jpg/250px-Usher_Looking_To_Obama.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_Looking_To_Obama.jpg)

Usher performing at the [We Are One: The Obama Inaugural Celebration at the Lincoln Memorial](/wiki/We_Are_One:_The_Obama_Inaugural_Celebration_at_the_Lincoln_Memorial "We Are One: The Obama Inaugural Celebration at the Lincoln Memorial") in 2009

*[Here I Stand](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album) "Here I Stand (Usher album)")* was released on May 26 in the United Kingdom and May 27, 2008, in the United States. The album debuted at No. 1 on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") Chart with first-week sales of over 433,000 copies.[[69]](#cite_note-HastyDebut-69) To date *Here I Stand* has sold over 1.5 million copies in the United States,[[70]](#cite_note-70) received a double platinum certification from the RIAA,[[71]](#cite_note-71) and has sold over 5 million copies worldwide.[[72]](#cite_note-72) While not approaching the success of his previous album, it received positive reviews from most music critics, who praised the maturity in the album's lyrics. To promote Usher's fifth studio album, the single "[Love In This Club](/wiki/Love_In_This_Club "Love In This Club")" was sent to radio in February 2008 and peaked at number one on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"). It went on to spend three consecutive weeks at the top—becoming Usher's eighth number-one single and the fastest-rising song of his career.[[69]](#cite_note-HastyDebut-69)[[73]](#cite_note-73) It also reached No. 1 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart. The single was another huge international success for Usher. It reached No. 1 on the [New Zealand Singles Chart](/wiki/Recording_Industry_Association_of_New_Zealand "Recording Industry Association of New Zealand"), No. 3 on the [Irish Singles Chart](/wiki/Irish_Singles_Chart "Irish Singles Chart"), No. 3 on the [Eurochart Hot 100](/wiki/Eurochart_Hot_100 "Eurochart Hot 100"), No. 4 on the [UK Singles Chart](/wiki/UK_Singles_Chart "UK Singles Chart"), No. 5 on the [Japan Hot 100](/wiki/Japan_Hot_100 "Japan Hot 100"), No. 5 on the [German Singles Chart](/wiki/German_Singles_Chart "German Singles Chart"), No. 5 on the [Belgian Singles Chart](/wiki/Ultratop_50 "Ultratop 50") (Flanders), No. 6 on the [Canadian Hot 100](/wiki/Canadian_Hot_100 "Canadian Hot 100"), No. 8 on the [Swedish Singles Chart](/wiki/Sverigetopplistan "Sverigetopplistan"), No. 8 on the [Australian Singles Chart](/wiki/ARIA_Charts "ARIA Charts"), No. 9 on the [French Singles Chart](/wiki/French_Singles_Chart "French Singles Chart"), No. 9 on the [Swiss Singles Chart](/wiki/Swiss_Singles_Chart "Swiss Singles Chart"), No. 10 on the [Norwegian Singles Chart](/wiki/Norwegian_Singles_Chart "Norwegian Singles Chart"), No. 12 on the [Austrian Singles Chart](/wiki/%C3%963_Austria_Top_40 "Ö3 Austria Top 40"), No. 13 on the [Belgian Singles Chart](/wiki/Ultratop_50 "Ultratop 50") (Wallonia), and No. 18 on the [Finnish Singles Chart](/wiki/Yle "Yle"). The follow-up single "[Love in This Club Part II](/wiki/Love_in_This_Club_Part_II "Love in This Club Part II")", which features American singer [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé") and rapper [Lil Wayne](/wiki/Lil_Wayne "Lil Wayne"), peaked at No. 18 on the *Billboard* Hot 100 and No. 7 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart. Its third single "[Moving Mountains](/wiki/Moving_Mountains_(song) "Moving Mountains (song)")" peaked at No. 18 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart and reached No. 6 on the [New Zealand Singles Chart](/wiki/New_Zealand_Singles_Chart "New Zealand Singles Chart"). The album's fifth single "[Trading Places](/wiki/Trading_Places_(song) "Trading Places (song)")" peaked at No. 4 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart.[[32]](#cite_note-BillboardChart-32) In September 2008, Usher announced he would embark on the 15-date tour *One Night Stand*, in which the audience is only females.[[74]](#cite_note-74) On January 18, 2009, Usher performed with [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder") and [Shakira](/wiki/Shakira "Shakira") at the [We Are One: The Obama Inaugural Celebration at the Lincoln Memorial](/wiki/We_Are_One:_The_Obama_Inaugural_Celebration_at_the_Lincoln_Memorial "We Are One: The Obama Inaugural Celebration at the Lincoln Memorial"). He also sang "[Gone Too Soon](/wiki/Gone_Too_Soon "Gone Too Soon")" at the [memorial](/wiki/Michael_Jackson_memorial_service "Michael Jackson memorial service") of [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") on July 7, 2009.[[75]](#cite_note-75)

### 2010–2011: *Raymond v. Raymond* and *Versus*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/af/Usher_at_Madison_Square_Garden_by_Loren_Wohl.jpg/250px-Usher_at_Madison_Square_Garden_by_Loren_Wohl.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_at_Madison_Square_Garden_by_Loren_Wohl.jpg)

Usher on the [OMG Tour](/wiki/OMG_Tour "OMG Tour") in 2010

*[Raymond v. Raymond](/wiki/Raymond_v._Raymond "Raymond v. Raymond")* was released on March 26, 2010, in Germany, March 30, 2010, in the US, and April 26, 2010, in the UK.[[76]](#cite_note-76) The album was expected to follow in Usher's *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* album's footsteps. *Raymond v. Raymond* was released only months after Usher's divorce from Tameka Foster. "[Papers](/wiki/Papers_(song) "Papers (song)")", a song about divorce,[[77]](#cite_note-77) was released as the first single for the album in October 2009. It topped the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart for two consecutive weeks, becoming his tenth number one single on that chart. It also peaked at number 31 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Hot_100 "Hot 100") in the United States. Critics praised the song for its emotion. "[Hey Daddy (Daddy's Home)](/wiki/Hey_Daddy_(Daddy%27s_Home) "Hey Daddy (Daddy's Home)")" was released as the second single on December 8, 2009. The single peaked at number 24[[78]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-78) on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Hot_100 "Hot 100") chart and number 2[[79]](#cite_note-79) on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart. The song was released as the second international single in July 2010. "[Lil Freak](/wiki/Lil_Freak "Lil Freak")" was announced as the album's official second single in the United States. Usher and [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj") shot the music video for the song on March 9, 2010, in Los Angeles with director TAJ Stansberry.[[80]](#cite_note-80) It reached number 8 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart[[81]](#cite_note-81) and number 40 on the *Billboard* Hot 100.[[82]](#cite_note-82) It became Usher's fourth Top 40 hit single from *Raymond v. Raymond*, when including the buzz single "Papers".[[83]](#cite_note-83)

"[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")", which features [will.i.am](/wiki/Will.i.am "Will.i.am"), is the third official US single and the first international single. The song received mixed reviews, complimenting the song's dance and club vibe but criticizing the [Auto-Tune](/wiki/Auto-Tune "Auto-Tune") effect. It reached number-one in Ireland, New Zealand, the United Kingdom, Australia and the United States. The song became his ninth number one in the United States, making him the first 2010s artist to collect number one singles in three consecutive decades, and only the fourth artist of all-time to achieve the feat. Usher also became the third artist to have at least one number one song from five consecutive studio albums. The song's choreography and dance-heavy accompanying music video has been compared to that of "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")". "[There Goes My Baby](/wiki/There_Goes_My_Baby_(Usher_song) "There Goes My Baby (Usher song)")" was released to airplay as the album's fourth single in the United States on June 15, 2010.[[84]](#cite_note-84) The song reached number 25 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), and number-one on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart, becoming Usher's eleventh number-one hit on that chart. The song ended up tying with "[You Make Me Wanna](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")" for fourth longest stay on the chart with 71 weeks. All of the album's singles received very heavy air play. On April 7, 2010, Raymond v. Raymond debuted at number No. 1 on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart, becoming his third consecutive No. 1 album and selling an impressive 329,107 copies in its first week of release,[[85]](#cite_note-blogs.bet.com-85) making him the first male artist since [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem") to have three consecutive albums debut at number one on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart. One month after being released, the album was certified Gold by the RIAA.[[86]](#cite_note-86) On February 9, 2024, the album was certified three-times Platinum by the RIAA.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

*Raymond v. Raymond* also dominated the International Charts, debuting inside the top 10 in Canada, the United Kingdom, Holland, Australia, Germany, Spain, and Italy. Due to the huge international success of Usher's single "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")" and the good first week sales for *Raymond v. Raymond,* the album reached number two in Australia and has been certified gold by the [Australian Recording Industry Association](/wiki/Australian_Recording_Industry_Association "Australian Recording Industry Association") (ARIA).[[87]](#cite_note-ariacharts1-87) The album debuted at number four in Canada[[88]](#cite_note-88) and has been certified Gold by the [Canadian Recording Industry Association](/wiki/Canadian_Recording_Industry_Association "Canadian Recording Industry Association") (CRIA).[[89]](#cite_note-89) *Raymond v. Raymond* debuted at number-two in the United Kingdom.[[90]](#cite_note-theofficialcharts1-90)

Usher announced on July 8, 2010,[[91]](#cite_note-independent.co.uk-91) a follow-up [extended play](/wiki/Extended_play "Extended play") to his sixth studio album *Raymond v. Raymond* called *[Versus](/wiki/Versus_(Usher_EP) "Versus (Usher EP)")*, and a deluxe edition of *Raymond v. Raymond*, both to be released on August 24, 2010.[[92]](#cite_note-RapUp1-92) Usher described *Versus* as "the last chapter of *[Raymond v. Raymond](/wiki/Raymond_v._Raymond "Raymond v. Raymond")*", and that it would explore the subjects of being newly single and a father.[[91]](#cite_note-independent.co.uk-91) The album included 9 tracks, including 7 new tracks, *Raymond v. Raymond* single "[There Goes My Baby](/wiki/There_Goes_My_Baby_(Usher_song) "There Goes My Baby (Usher song)")", and [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber") single "[Somebody to Love (Remix)](/wiki/Somebody_to_Love_(Justin_Bieber_song) "Somebody to Love (Justin Bieber song)")". The tracks would be included on a deluxe edition of *Raymond v. Raymond*. The album debuted at number four on the [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200") chart and is preceded by the singles "DJ Got Us Fallin' In Love" (featuring [Pitbull](/wiki/Pitbull_(entertainer) "Pitbull (entertainer)")), for mainstream audiences, and "Hot Tottie" (featuring [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z")), for urban circuits.[[93]](#cite_note-93)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/ba/Usher_OMG_Houston.jpg/250px-Usher_OMG_Houston.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_OMG_Houston.jpg)

Usher performing in 2010

The first single from the album, "[DJ Got Us Fallin' In Love](/wiki/DJ_Got_Us_Fallin%27_In_Love "DJ Got Us Fallin' In Love")", was released to [iTunes](/wiki/ITunes "ITunes") on July 13, 2010, and sent to radio on July 20, 2010. Due to strong digital sales the song debuted at number nineteen on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100").[[94]](#cite_note-94) The song became the fourth highest debut on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") of his career, behind 1997's "Nice & Slow" at number nine, 1998's "My Way" at eight, and 2010's "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")" at number fourteen. Since its release, it has gained international success, peaking in the top 5 in the United States, Australia, and New Zealand. It reached the top 10 in Canada and Europe. It reached number-nine on the Billboard Hot 100 in its third week of release, and became the first time Usher has had two top-ten hits on the *Billboard* Hot 100 at the same time since his album *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")*.[[78]](#cite_note-ReferenceA-78) It also became Usher's sixteenth Billboard Hot 100 top-ten hit of his career. The second single, [Hot Tottie](/wiki/Hot_Tottie "Hot Tottie"), reached number nine on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart[[95]](#cite_note-95) and number twenty-one on the Billboard Hot 100.[[96]](#cite_note-96)

Usher appeared at the [2010 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2010_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2010 MTV Video Music Awards") on September 12, 2010.[[97]](#cite_note-97) He then performed at the 2010 American Music Awards on November 21, 2010, and also won the awards for Male Soul/R&B artist and Favorite Soul/R&B album for his album *Raymond v. Raymond*.[[98]](#cite_note-98) According to Rap-Up.com, Usher started working on his next studio album,[[99]](#cite_note-newlp-99) again teaming up with long-time collaborator [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love").[[99]](#cite_note-newlp-99) The [OMG Tour](/wiki/OMG_Tour "OMG Tour") began in November 2010[[100]](#cite_note-100) and ended on June 1, 2011. On February 6, 2011, Usher also made an appearance in the [Super Bowl XLV halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_XLV_halftime_show "Super Bowl XLV halftime show") to sing his song "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")" with [The Black Eyed Peas](/wiki/The_Black_Eyed_Peas "The Black Eyed Peas")' singer [will.i.am](/wiki/Will.i.am "Will.i.am").[[101]](#cite_note-101)

On September 8, 2011, Usher appeared on [Romeo Santos](/wiki/Romeo_Santos "Romeo Santos")' second single "[Promise](/wiki/Promise_(Romeo_Santos_song) "Promise (Romeo Santos song)")" from his debut studio album *[Formula, Vol. 1](/wiki/Formula,_Vol._1 "Formula, Vol. 1").* The song was his first number-one on the [*Billboard* Latin Songs](/wiki/Hot_Latin_Songs "Hot Latin Songs") chart. A month later, on October 7, Sony Music, which was a parent company to the [RCA Music Group](/wiki/RCA_Music_Group "RCA Music Group"), confirmed the consolidation of Usher's previous labels, Jive, and Arista, along with [J Records](/wiki/J_Records "J Records").[[102]](#cite_note-102) Following the consolidation of the three labels, Usher (and various other artists previously signed to the three aforementioned labels) were deferred to release planned material for [RCA Records](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records").[[103]](#cite_note-103)

### 2012–2014: *Looking 4 Myself* and *The Voice*

In November 2011, Usher revealed that for his next album he was working on a new type of music which he called "revolutionary pop", which combined different genres to form a new sound.[[104]](#cite_note-104) His seventh studio album *[Looking 4 Myself](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself "Looking 4 Myself")* was released on June 8, 2012, worldwide, and received generally positive reviews from contemporary music critics.[[105]](#cite_note-itunes-105)[[106]](#cite_note-Metacritic-106) American singer-songwriter and record producer [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love") was interviewed by *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* magazine, where he spoke about his relationships with artists and experiences. He commented on how Usher wanted to do things differently on the album compared to his previous efforts, saying: "What he wanted to do [on *Looking 4 Myself*] was explore himself musically. He stepped outside of what was safe and normal. He wanted to make an album that expressed where he was going sonically and not just where he's been for the past 12 to 15 years. He's growing, developing, moving, shaking, and being something that's new, [cultural](/wiki/Cultural "Cultural"), and that's affecting people sonically. That's kind of forcing the people to grow and elevate".[[107]](#cite_note-107) In an interview for [MTV News](/wiki/MTV_News "MTV News"), Usher stated that *Looking 4 Myself* has "a lot there to keep fans tuning in".[[108]](#cite_note-108) When questioned by [Reuters](/wiki/Reuters "Reuters") during an interview regarding the latter quote, and how this project was different, Usher explained that he felt he was near a "rebirth" and that prior to *Looking 4 Myself*, he felt restricted and conformed to a specific standard. He said to himself "I gotta go with what I feel and hopefully people will follow me".[[109]](#cite_note-rebirth-109)

"[Climax](/wiki/Climax_(Usher_song) "Climax (Usher song)")" served as the lead single from his seventh studio album. It impacted Urban radio on February 21, 2012, and was made available for purchase as a [digital download](/wiki/Music_download "Music download") on February 22, 2012. It impacted US mainstream radio on March 13, 2012.[[110]](#cite_note-110)[[111]](#cite_note-111) The second single from *Looking 4 Myself* was "[Scream](/wiki/Scream_(Usher_song) "Scream (Usher song)")" and the third single "Lemme See" which features Rick Ross. "Scream" was premiered on April 26, 2012, while "Lemme See" was premiered on May 8, 2012.[[112]](#cite_note-112) On *Billboard* [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Airplay "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay"), "Climax" enabled Usher to add a second song of his in the six longest stays at number one for 11 weeks after "[You Make Me Wanna...](/wiki/You_Make_Me_Wanna... "You Make Me Wanna...")" with 12 weeks.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2013, Usher substituted for [CeeLo Green](/wiki/CeeLo_Green "CeeLo Green") as a coach for the [fourth season](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._season_4) "The Voice (U.S. season 4)") of [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._TV_series) "The Voice (U.S. TV series)")*. His last act, [Michelle Chamuel](/wiki/Michelle_Chamuel "Michelle Chamuel"), lost the winning title to [Danielle Bradbery](/wiki/Danielle_Bradbery "Danielle Bradbery"), mentored by [Blake Shelton](/wiki/Blake_Shelton "Blake Shelton").[[113]](#cite_note-113) He returned for the [sixth season](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._season_6) "The Voice (U.S. season 6)") and his last act, [Josh Kaufman](/wiki/Josh_Kaufman_(singer) "Josh Kaufman (singer)"), won.[[114]](#cite_note-114)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/bb/Usher_SAP_Arena_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Usher_SAP_Arena_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_SAP_Arena_(cropped).jpg)

Usher on the [URX Tour](/wiki/UR_Experience_Tour "UR Experience Tour") in 2014

On March 19, 2013, Usher announced that his eighth studio album, titled *UR*, was in the works.[[115]](#cite_note-next_album1-115) Talking to *[The Fader](/wiki/The_Fader "The Fader")*, he described the album as "everything you can imagine", saying that it's "gonna be freaking out of here".[[115]](#cite_note-next_album1-115) Singer-songwriter Eric Bellinger told *[Rap-Up](/wiki/Rap-Up "Rap-Up")* that he, along with [Jermaine Dupri](/wiki/Jermaine_Dupri "Jermaine Dupri"), [Bryan-Michael Cox](/wiki/Bryan-Michael_Cox "Bryan-Michael Cox"), and [Brian Alexander Morgan](/wiki/Brian_Alexander_Morgan "Brian Alexander Morgan"), was working on Usher's next album.[[116]](#cite_note-next_album-116) Bellinger compared the album's music to Usher's *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* (2004), saying that it is "more R&B, more urban" than Usher's *[Looking 4 Myself](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself "Looking 4 Myself")*.[[116]](#cite_note-next_album-116) The latter declared that his next album would show that he is "still Usher".[[116]](#cite_note-next_album-116) "[Good Kisser](/wiki/Good_Kisser "Good Kisser")", a dance-heavy throwback R&B track, was released as the first single allowing Usher to achieve his 13th no.1 on the [Airplay](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Airplay "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay") chart, tying [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(rapper) "Drake (rapper)") for most leaders in the list's history.[[117]](#cite_note-117) Produced by Pop & Oak, it was performed at *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._TV_series) "The Voice (U.S. TV series)")*.[[118]](#cite_note-118) Since then, two other singles have been released from the album: "[She Came to Give It to You](/wiki/She_Came_to_Give_It_to_You "She Came to Give It to You")" (featuring [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj")) and "[I Don't Mind](/wiki/I_Don%27t_Mind_(Usher_song) "I Don't Mind (Usher song)")" (featuring [Juicy J](/wiki/Juicy_J "Juicy J")). The latter allowed Usher to lead the [Rhythmic](/wiki/Rhythmic_(chart) "Rhythmic (chart)") charts with the most number ones (13).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On August 25, 2014, Usher announced his [UR Experience Tour](/wiki/UR_Experience_Tour "UR Experience Tour") in support of his then-titled eighth studio album *UR*. The UR Experience started in Montreal, Quebec on November 1 and ended in Tampa, Florida on December 14.[[119]](#cite_note-billboard.com-119) The 27 city North American leg was supported by opening acts [DJ Cassidy](/wiki/DJ_Cassidy "DJ Cassidy") and R&B newcomer [August Alsina](/wiki/August_Alsina "August Alsina"). The European leg of the tour visited 23 cities and the support would be [Nico & Vinz](/wiki/Nico_%26_Vinz "Nico & Vinz").[[120]](#cite_note-120) "With The UR Experience, I want to give my fans an ever-changing live show full of surprises and special guests," Usher says, via a press release. "I am really excited to be with my fans and give them an Usher experience like they've never seen or heard before."[[119]](#cite_note-billboard.com-119) While on tour, with the collaboration of [Honey Nut Cheerios](/wiki/Honey_Nut_Cheerios "Honey Nut Cheerios"), he released "Clueless", a new song that could be downloaded free using a code from specially-marked Honey Nut Cheerios boxes bought from Walmart.[[121]](#cite_note-121)

### 2015–2017: *Hard II Love* and *Hands of Stone*

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/d7/Usher_%2816571645134%29_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Usher_%2816571645134%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_(16571645134)_(cropped).jpg)

Usher at the [2015 Global Citizen Festival](/wiki/Global_Citizen_Festival#2015 "Global Citizen Festival")

On October 16, 2015, Usher released an interactive music video exclusively on the music streaming platform [Tidal](/wiki/Tidal_(service) "Tidal (service)") called "[Chains](/wiki/Chains_(Usher_song) "Chains (Usher song)")" (featuring [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas") and [Bibi Bourelly](/wiki/Bibi_Bourelly "Bibi Bourelly")). "Chains" literally forces the viewer to confront the issue of racial profiling and police brutality head-on: By utilizing the webcam on the viewer's computer as well as facial recognition technology, "Chains" paused mid-song whenever the viewer's eyes deviate from the video. Usher performed the song for the first time at the Tidal X:1020 concert on October 20, 2015, at Brooklyn's Barclays Arena.[[122]](#cite_note-122) On January 14, 2016, after continual delays of his eighth album, Usher announced via [Daniel Arsham](/wiki/Daniel_Arsham "Daniel Arsham") Instagram post that he changed the title of the album from *UR* to *Flawed*. It was scheduled to be released in April 2016, but has been pushed back with no release date.[[123]](#cite_note-123) On February 29, 2016, [Yuna](/wiki/Yuna_(Malaysian_singer) "Yuna (Malaysian singer)") released "[Crush](/wiki/Crush_(Yuna_song) "Crush (Yuna song)")" as the first single from her forthcoming album [*Chapters*](/wiki/Chapters_(Yuna_album) "Chapters (Yuna album)") featuring Usher.[[124]](#cite_note-124) On June 9, 2016, Usher released a new single from his expected forthcoming album *Flawed* on the music streaming platform [Tidal](/wiki/Tidal_(service) "Tidal (service)") titled "[No Limit](/wiki/No_Limit_(Usher_song) "No Limit (Usher song)")" (featuring [Young Thug](/wiki/Young_Thug "Young Thug")), along with a second single titled "[Crash](/wiki/Crash_(Usher_song) "Crash (Usher song)")" on June 10 for all digital platforms.[[125]](#cite_note-125)

On June 12, 2016, it was reported that Raymond signed a management deal with business partner [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun"). Braun already represented Justin Bieber and had a joint venture with Raymond and Bieber.[[126]](#cite_note-126) This report was confirmed by Usher during a sit down interview with [Ryan Seacrest](/wiki/Ryan_Seacrest "Ryan Seacrest") at the 73rd annual [Cannes Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Lions_International_Festival_of_Creativity "Cannes Lions International Festival of Creativity") on June 21, 2016.[[127]](#cite_note-127) Usher furthered his acting career, starring as American former professional boxer [Sugar Ray Leonard](/wiki/Sugar_Ray_Leonard "Sugar Ray Leonard") in the 2016 American-Panamanian biographical sports film *[Hands of Stone](/wiki/Hands_of_Stone "Hands of Stone")* about the career of Panamanian former professional boxer [Roberto Durán](/wiki/Roberto_Dur%C3%A1n "Roberto Durán"). In it, he starred opposite of [Édgar Ramírez](/wiki/%C3%89dgar_Ram%C3%ADrez "Édgar Ramírez"), [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro"), and [Ruben Blades](/wiki/Rub%C3%A9n_Blades "Rubén Blades"). On August 26, 2016, *Hands of Stone* premiered worldwide.[[128]](#cite_note-128) "[Missin U](/wiki/Missin_U "Missin U")" was also released as a third single by [RCA Records](/wiki/RCA_Records "RCA Records") for online streaming and digital download, along with "[Champions](/wiki/Champions_(Usher_song) "Champions (Usher song)") (from the Motion Picture Hands of Stone)", both on [Vevo](/wiki/Vevo "Vevo").[[129]](#cite_note-129) On August 30, "[Rivals](/wiki/Rivals_(Usher_song) "Rivals (Usher song)")" (featuring [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)")) was released and debuted exclusively on Tidal with its accompanying video.[[130]](#cite_note-130) It was released on Vevo on September 2, 2016.[[131]](#cite_note-131) On September 13, 2016, both "Missin U" and "Rivals" were sent to radio as the album's third and fourth singles.[[132]](#cite_note-132)

The album *[Hard II Love](/wiki/Hard_II_Love "Hard II Love")* was released on September 16, 2016, and received generally positive reviews from music critics. It debuted at number 5 on the US [*Billboard* 200](/wiki/Billboard_200 "Billboard 200"), and sold 28,000 copies in the United States in its first week.[[133]](#cite_note-133) It also became his seventh consecutive top ten album in the United States. The lead single, "No Limit" initially was serviced to Urban radio on June 9, and eventually peaked at number 1 on the [Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop](/wiki/Mainstream_R%26B/Hip-Hop "Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop") airplay chart, number 32 on the [*Billboard* Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100"), and number 9 on the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs").[[134]](#cite_note-134)[[135]](#cite_note-135) On December 16, 2016, [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown") released "[Party](/wiki/Party_(Chris_Brown_song) "Party (Chris Brown song)")" as the second single from his album *[Heartbreak on a Full Moon](/wiki/Heartbreak_on_a_Full_Moon "Heartbreak on a Full Moon")* featuring Usher and [Gucci Mane](/wiki/Gucci_Mane "Gucci Mane").[[136]](#cite_note-136)

### 2018–2020: *A* and return to *The Voice*

On October 12, 2018, Usher released a collaborative studio album, titled simply *[A](/wiki/A_(Usher_and_Zaytoven_album) "A (Usher and Zaytoven album)")*. The official tracklist contains 8 songs with features from [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)") and [Gunna](/wiki/Gunna_(rapper) "Gunna (rapper)").[[137]](#cite_note-137) The album was produced entirely by [Zaytoven](/wiki/Zaytoven "Zaytoven"), who previously collaborated on Usher's *[Raymond v. Raymond](/wiki/Raymond_v._Raymond "Raymond v. Raymond")* album as a co-producer for the single "[Papers](/wiki/Papers_(song) "Papers (song)")".[[138]](#cite_note-138) Between November 9–17, 2018, Usher served as the headliner for the [RNB Fridays Live](/wiki/RNB_Fridays_Live "RNB Fridays Live") concert series. The six date stadium tour in [Australia](/wiki/Australia "Australia") and [New Zealand](/wiki/New_Zealand "New Zealand") sold over 150,000 tickets[[139]](#cite_note-139) On November 14, 2018, [*Billboard*](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)") reported that Usher signed a worldwide management deal with Luke Mitzman and 100 Management. It was also reported he is working on a follow up album that will be released in 2019.[[140]](#cite_note-140) On December 2, 2018, Usher performed at the [Global Citizen Festival: Mandela 100](/wiki/Global_Citizen_Festival "Global Citizen Festival") in [Johannesburg](/wiki/Johannesburg "Johannesburg"), [South Africa](/wiki/South_Africa "South Africa"), celebrating the legacy of [Nelson Mandela](/wiki/Nelson_Mandela "Nelson Mandela"). Usher and DJ [Black Coffee](/wiki/Black_Coffee_(DJ) "Black Coffee (DJ)") performed a mash-up featuring the South African Indigenous Dance Academy (IDA).[[141]](#cite_note-141)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/6/64/First_Lady_Melania_Trump_at_the_Kennedy_Center_%2848688952977%29_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:First_Lady_Melania_Trump_at_the_Kennedy_Center_(48688952977)_(cropped).jpg)

Usher at the [Kennedy Center](/wiki/Kennedy_Center "Kennedy Center") in 2019

Usher had a cameo in the crime film *[Hustlers](/wiki/Hustlers_(film) "Hustlers (film)")*, which was released on September 13, 2019, and stars [Jennifer Lopez](/wiki/Jennifer_Lopez "Jennifer Lopez"), [Constance Wu](/wiki/Constance_Wu "Constance Wu"), [Keke Palmer](/wiki/Keke_Palmer "Keke Palmer"), [Cardi B](/wiki/Cardi_B "Cardi B"), and [Lili Reinhart](/wiki/Lili_Reinhart "Lili Reinhart").[[142]](#cite_note-142) In September 2019, director [Lorene Scafaria](/wiki/Lorene_Scafaria "Lorene Scafaria") told [Vulture](/wiki/Vulture_(website) "Vulture (website)") magazine his cameo was written into the script in 2016, before the film went into development.[[143]](#cite_note-143) Usher is featured on the film's soundtrack, with his single "[Love in This Club](/wiki/Love_in_This_Club "Love in This Club")".[[144]](#cite_note-144) In March 2019, Usher posted a photo of himself in the studio next to a whiteboard with the title "Confessions 2" and a blurred out tracklisting.[[145]](#cite_note-145) The following day producer [Jermaine Dupri](/wiki/Jermaine_Dupri "Jermaine Dupri"), who produced the original *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* went live on Instagram and previewed new music. On June 15, 2019, Usher, alongside [Erykah Badu](/wiki/Erykah_Badu "Erykah Badu"), headlined the second annual Smoking Grooves Festival at the Queen Mary Events Park in [Long Beach, California](/wiki/Long_Beach,_California "Long Beach, California").[[146]](#cite_note-146)

On September 6, 2019, Usher returned for [season seventeen](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_season_17) "The Voice (American season 17)") of [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s *[The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(American_TV_series) "The Voice (American TV series)").* He joined [John Legend](/wiki/John_Legend "John Legend") team as this season's battle advisor.[[147]](#cite_note-147) On September 10, 2019, DJ [Black Coffee](/wiki/Black_Coffee_(DJ) "Black Coffee (DJ)") released his single "[LaLaLa](/wiki/LaLaLa_(Black_Coffee_and_Usher_song) "LaLaLa (Black Coffee and Usher song)")" with Usher from his forthcoming album.[[148]](#cite_note-148) It reached number one on the South Africa airplay charts.[[149]](#cite_note-149) Usher appeared on [Summer Walker](/wiki/Summer_Walker "Summer Walker")'s debut album *[Over It](/wiki/Over_It_(album) "Over It (album)")*, which was released on October 4, 2019.[[150]](#cite_note-150) They collaborated on "[Come Thru](/wiki/Come_Thru "Come Thru")", which samples his song "You Make Me Wanna..." Critics from *[Time](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)")*, *Complex*, and *[Paper](/wiki/Paper_(magazine) "Paper (magazine)")* all named it one of the best songs of the week.[[151]](#cite_note-151)[[152]](#cite_note-152)[[153]](#cite_note-153)

On December 13, 2019, Usher released the lead single, "[Don't Waste My Time](/wiki/Don%27t_Waste_My_Time_(Usher_song) "Don't Waste My Time (Usher song)")" (featuring [Ella Mai](/wiki/Ella_Mai "Ella Mai")) for his upcoming ninth studio album. It was co-produced by longtime collaborators [Bryan-Michael Cox](/wiki/Bryan-Michael_Cox "Bryan-Michael Cox") and Jermaine Dupri.[[154]](#cite_note-154) Usher, Ella Mai, and Jermaine Dupri performed the song on [BET](/wiki/BET "BET")'s *[Saving Our Selves](/wiki/Saving_Our_Selves "Saving Our Selves")*, to benefit [African American](/wiki/African_Americans "African Americans") communities impacted by the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic") on April 22, 2020.[[155]](#cite_note-155) It topped the [Adult R&B Chart](/wiki/Adult_R%26B_Songs "Adult R&B Songs"), becoming his fifth number one single on that chart.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Usher performed at the [62nd Annual Grammy Awards](/wiki/62nd_Annual_Grammy_Awards "62nd Annual Grammy Awards") on January 26, 2020.[[156]](#cite_note-156) Usher and [Sheila E.](/wiki/Sheila_E. "Sheila E.") performed a special tribute honoring [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)"). This performance was teaser for the *Let's Go Crazy: The Grammy Salute to Prince* concert at the [Staples Center](/wiki/Crypto.com_Arena "Crypto.com Arena") scheduled for January 28, 2020 on CBS featuring performances from [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys"), [John Legend](/wiki/John_Legend "John Legend"), [Chris Martin](/wiki/Chris_Martin "Chris Martin"), [H.E.R.](/wiki/H.E.R. "H.E.R."), and more. On January 31, to initiate a tribute for [Kobe Bryant](/wiki/Kobe_Bryant "Kobe Bryant"), who was killed five days earlier in a helicopter crash alongside his 13-year-old daughter [Gianna](/wiki/Gianna_Bryant "Gianna Bryant") and seven others, Usher performed "[Amazing Grace](/wiki/Amazing_Grace "Amazing Grace")" at the Staples Center prior a game between the [Los Angeles Lakers](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Lakers "Los Angeles Lakers") and [Portland Trail Blazers](/wiki/Portland_Trail_Blazers "Portland Trail Blazers"), in what was the Lakers' first game played since the tragedy.[[157]](#cite_note-157) Usher had been set to host and perform at the [2020 iHeartRadio Music Awards](/wiki/2020_iHeartRadio_Music_Awards "2020 iHeartRadio Music Awards"), which had been set scheduled to broadcast live on [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") on March 29.[[158]](#cite_note-158) The event was postponed due to concerns over the spread of [COVID-19](/wiki/COVID-19 "COVID-19").[[159]](#cite_note-159)

Usher served as executive producer and judge on a dance competition series called *The Sauce*. The seven-episode series premiered on April 6, 2020, on the streaming service [Quibi](/wiki/Quibi "Quibi").[[160]](#cite_note-160) On April 10, 2020, Usher released a single, "[SexBeat](/wiki/SexBeat "SexBeat")", with former collaborators [Lil Jon](/wiki/Lil_Jon "Lil Jon") and [Ludacris](/wiki/Ludacris "Ludacris").[[161]](#cite_note-161) It was co-written by Jermaine Dupri and Antoine Harris. The release of "SexBeat" came days after Lil Jon premiered the track on April 5 during an online music battle called *[Verzuz](/wiki/Verzuz "Verzuz")* hosted by [Swizz Beatz](/wiki/Swizz_Beatz "Swizz Beatz") and [Timbaland](/wiki/Timbaland "Timbaland").[[162]](#cite_note-162) He appeared on [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC")'s *[Songland](/wiki/Songland "Songland")* and released the song "[California](/wiki/California_(Usher_song) "California (Usher song)")" (featuring [Tyga](/wiki/Tyga "Tyga")) on June 15, 2020.[[163]](#cite_note-163) On June 26, 2020, Usher released "[I Cry](/wiki/I_Cry_(Usher_song) "I Cry (Usher song)")".[[164]](#cite_note-164)

### 2021–present: Residencies, *Coming Home*, and Super Bowl LVIII halftime show

On September 4, 2020, Usher announced he would be headlining a 12 show residency at the [Colosseum at Caesars' Palace](/wiki/The_Colosseum_at_Caesars_Palace "The Colosseum at Caesars Palace") in [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas"), [Nevada](/wiki/Nevada "Nevada").[[165]](#cite_note-165) The show was scheduled to start on July 16, 2021, and slated to run until January 1, 2022. Live Nation Las Vegas and Caesars Entertainment will be donating $1 from every ticket purchased to [Usher's New Look](/wiki/Usher%27s_New_Look "Usher's New Look") foundation.[[166]](#cite_note-166) In December 2021, [Bruno Mars](/wiki/Bruno_Mars "Bruno Mars") announced onstage with Usher that he was moving his residency at [Dolby Live at Park MGM](/wiki/Park_MGM "Park MGM").[[167]](#cite_note-167) On February 17, 2022, Usher officially announced that he would be moving his residency from Caesars Palace to Park MGM and would be headlining 23 shows.[[168]](#cite_note-168) Usher released the single "[Bad Habits](/wiki/Bad_Habits_(Usher_song) "Bad Habits (Usher song)")" on September 10, 2020, as the second single off his forthcoming studio album. A music video directed by [Chris Robinson](/wiki/Chris_Robinson_(director) "Chris Robinson (director)") accompanied the song.[[169]](#cite_note-169) It topped the [Adult R&B Chart](/wiki/Adult_R%26B_Songs "Adult R&B Songs"), becoming his sixth number one single on that chart.[[170]](#cite_note-170)

On March 8, 2022, it was announced that Usher had parted ways with RCA Records after ten years and five months with the imprint following the RCA and Sony Music Group merger. He later signed a joint venture with [Larry Jackson](/wiki/Larry_Jackson_(music_executive) "Larry Jackson (music executive)")'s [Gamma](/wiki/Gamma_(media_company) "Gamma (media company)") music company.[[171]](#cite_note-171) Usher and his longtime partner, [L.A. Reid](/wiki/L.A._Reid "L.A. Reid"), founded Mega in partnership with Gamma. Usher's eighth studio album will be released through this partnership.[[172]](#cite_note-172) On June 30, 2022, Usher performed on [NPR](/wiki/NPR "NPR")'s *[Tiny Desk Concerts](/wiki/List_of_Tiny_Desk_Concerts "List of Tiny Desk Concerts")* series. He was joined by vocalists [Eric Bellinger](/wiki/Eric_Bellinger "Eric Bellinger") and Vedo, bassist, Dmitry Gorodetsky, trombonist Lemar Guillary, trumpeter Brandyn Phillips, saxophonist Jay Flat, keyboardist Darek Cobbs, guitarist Erick Walls and drummer Ryan Carr.[[173]](#cite_note-173) A clip from the performance of Usher whispering "watch this" went [viral](/wiki/Viral_video "Viral video") as an [internet meme](/wiki/Internet_meme "Internet meme").[[174]](#cite_note-174)[[175]](#cite_note-175) On March 17, 2023, Usher released the single "[Glu](/wiki/Glu_(song) "Glu (song)")", his first solo single in over two years.[[176]](#cite_note-176)[[177]](#cite_note-177)[[178]](#cite_note-178) It was co-produced by longtime collaborators Lil Jon, The Avila Brothers, and [Sean Garrett](/wiki/Sean_Garrett "Sean Garrett"). It topped the Adult R&B Chart, becoming his seventh number one single on that chart. On April 14, 2023, Usher announced his 8-night show residency in [Boulogne-Billancourt](/wiki/Boulogne-Billancourt "Boulogne-Billancourt"), titled "Rendez-Vous À Paris" at [La Seine Musicale](/wiki/La_Seine_Musicale "La Seine Musicale") presented by Live Nation Entertainment. The residency starts on September 24 and runs through to October 5.[[179]](#cite_note-179) On August 4, 2023, Usher released the single "[Good Good](/wiki/Good_Good_(Usher,_Summer_Walker_and_21_Savage_song) "Good Good (Usher, Summer Walker and 21 Savage song)")" with [Summer Walker](/wiki/Summer_Walker "Summer Walker") and [21 Savage](/wiki/21_Savage "21 Savage") as the lead single to his upcoming ninth studio album.[[180]](#cite_note-180) It topped the [R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay](/wiki/R%26B/Hip-Hop_Airplay "R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay") chart, becoming his 16th number one on that chart.[[181]](#cite_note-:2-181) It also topped the [Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop](/wiki/Mainstream_R%26B/Hip-Hop "Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop") airplay chart, becoming his seventeenth number one single on that chart.[[182]](#cite_note-Rutherford-182) He followed it up with a second single, "Boyfriend", released several weeks later. On September 14, 2023, he appeared on [J Balvin](/wiki/J_Balvin "J Balvin")'s single "Dientes". The song reached number-one on the [Latin Airplay](/wiki/Latin_Airplay "Latin Airplay") chart.[[183]](#cite_note-183)

On September 24, 2023, Usher announced that he would headline the [Super Bowl LVIII halftime show](/wiki/Super_Bowl_LVIII_halftime_show "Super Bowl LVIII halftime show"), held at the [Allegiant Stadium](/wiki/Allegiant_Stadium "Allegiant Stadium") in Las Vegas, Nevada.[[184]](#cite_note-184) He described the opportunity as "an honor of a lifetime" that he would finally be able to check off his bucket list.[[185]](#cite_note-185) In addition, he announced in an Apple Music interview with [Zane Lowe](/wiki/Zane_Lowe "Zane Lowe") that his ninth studio album, *[Coming Home](/wiki/Coming_Home_(Usher_album) "Coming Home (Usher album)")*, would be released on February 11, 2024, the same day it was reported that he would perform at the Super Bowl.[[186]](#cite_note-186)[[187]](#cite_note-187)[[188]](#cite_note-188) The album debuted at number two on the US *Billboard* 200.[[189]](#cite_note-:0-189) The album's second single, "Ruin" (featuring [Pheelz](/wiki/Pheelz "Pheelz")), was released on February 2, 2024, along with its accompanying music video. It was directed by [Dave Meyers](/wiki/Dave_Meyers_(director) "Dave Meyers (director)") and shot on an [iPhone 15 Pro](/wiki/IPhone_15_Pro "IPhone 15 Pro").[[190]](#cite_note-190) On February 6, Usher announced the [Usher: Past Present Future](/wiki/Usher:_Past_Present_Future "Usher: Past Present Future") 24-stop tour, which will visit [North America](/wiki/North_America "North America") and [Europe](/wiki/Europe "Europe"). Due to overwhelming demand, it expanded from 24 to 58 shows.[[191]](#cite_note-191)[[192]](#cite_note-192) On June 30, 2024, Usher was honored with the Lifetime Achievement Award at the [24th BET Awards](/wiki/BET_Awards_2024 "BET Awards 2024") for his notable contributions to the music industry.[[193]](#cite_note-193) In October 2024, Usher was named the 11th "[Greatest Pop Star of the 21st Century](/wiki/Billboard%27s_Greatest_Pop_Stars_of_the_21st_Century "Billboard's Greatest Pop Stars of the 21st Century")" by *Billboard*.[[194]](#cite_note-194)

## Artistry

### Musical style and influences

Usher's music is generally [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B"), but also incorporates [pop](/wiki/Pop_music "Pop music") among other genres.[[5]](#cite_note-NT-5)[[195]](#cite_note-195)[[196]](#cite_note-196) Elias Leight of *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* wrote that "catching pop's waves has always been one of Usher's greatest strengths, whether that means collaborating with Lil Jon when [crunk](/wiki/Crunk "Crunk") was going mainstream or charging into EDM".[[197]](#cite_note-197) *[Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)")* stated that Usher provided some of "the best of pop music in R&B".[[198]](#cite_note-198) His narratives have an autobiographical nature of emphasis on lifestyle, relationships and love,[[199]](#cite_note-Usher-199) resembling "an R&B [Casanova](/wiki/Casanova "Casanova")".[[200]](#cite_note-Jody_Rosen-200)[[201]](#cite_note-NY-201) *[Usher](/wiki/Usher_(album) "Usher (album)")* was [new jack swing](/wiki/New_jack_swing "New jack swing") oriented,[[202]](#cite_note-202) while *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_album) "My Way (Usher album)")* contained influences of [soul](/wiki/Soul_music "Soul music").[[203]](#cite_note-Exclaim-203) *[8701](/wiki/8701 "8701")* "introduces touches of '80s [rock guitar](/wiki/Rock_guitar "Rock guitar") and the minimal rhythm of '80s [hip hop](/wiki/Hip_hop_music "Hip hop music")" displaying versatility, according to [NME](/wiki/NME "NME")'s [Lucy O'Brien](/wiki/Lucy_O%27Brien "Lucy O'Brien").[[204]](#cite_note-204) *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* is viewed as a "top-of-the-line [pop-soul](/wiki/Soul_music#Detroit_(Motown) "Soul music") showcase",[[205]](#cite_note-205) with established critic [Robert Hilburn](/wiki/Robert_Hilburn "Robert Hilburn") of *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* seeing lead single "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")" as something [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") would have created "if he still had the old *[Thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(Michael_Jackson_album) "Thriller (Michael Jackson album)")* magic".[[206]](#cite_note-206) *[Here I Stand](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album) "Here I Stand (Usher album)")* was noted as more [ballad](/wiki/Sentimental_ballads "Sentimental ballads")-heavy by critics,[[207]](#cite_note-207) where the [title track](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_song) "Here I Stand (Usher song)")'s usage of [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") notably drew comparisons to [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder").[[208]](#cite_note-208) *[Raymond V Raymond](/wiki/Raymond_V_Raymond "Raymond V Raymond")* was described as a "skillful fusion of R&B/hip-hop/pop" by [Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)").[[209]](#cite_note-209) *[Looking 4 Myself](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself "Looking 4 Myself")* further explored progressive R&B, [hip hop ballads](/wiki/Hip_hop_soul "Hip hop soul"), and [synth pop](/wiki/Synth_pop "Synth pop"), with its overall sound coined "hybrid pop" for its dynamic incorporation of [EDM](/wiki/Electronic_dance_music "Electronic dance music"), [dubstep](/wiki/Dubstep "Dubstep"), [neo soul](/wiki/Neo_soul "Neo soul"), and [funk](/wiki/Funk "Funk").[[200]](#cite_note-Jody_Rosen-200)[[210]](#cite_note-LA_Times-210)[[211]](#cite_note-211) *[Hard II Love](/wiki/Hard_II_Love "Hard II Love")* sees *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")*'s [Maura Johnston](/wiki/Maura_Johnston "Maura Johnston") view it to "stretch the boundaries of R&B while winding toward the brooding atmospherics that have enveloped much of pop over the past 12 months".[[212]](#cite_note-212)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/de/Boyz_II_Men_-_Live_at_Vega_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Boyz_II_Men_-_Live_at_Vega_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Boyz_II_Men_-_Live_at_Vega_(cropped).jpg)

[Boyz II Men](/wiki/Boyz_II_Men "Boyz II Men") performing at [Las Vegas](/wiki/Las_Vegas "Las Vegas") in 2008

On many occasions, Usher has named [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") as his biggest influence. Usher once told [MTV](/wiki/Music_Television "Music Television"), "He influenced me in so many ways, more than just music ... as a humanitarian, as a philanthropist, as an artist, as an individual who transcended culture. I wouldn't be who I am today without Michael Jackson".[[213]](#cite_note-213) During the television special '[Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Special](/wiki/Michael_Jackson:_30th_Anniversary_Special "Michael Jackson: 30th Anniversary Special")', Usher was able to dance with Jackson while performing "[You Rock My World](/wiki/You_Rock_My_World "You Rock My World")".[[214]](#cite_note-214) [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)") being another main influence, Usher said "every album I'd always do like one song that is very Prince-influenced, because he's a great influence on me musically, especially just kind of being expansive and creating music that represents many different genres."[[215]](#cite_note-215) In [*Time*](/wiki/Time_(magazine) "Time (magazine)") magazine's "[100 Most Influential People](/wiki/100_Most_Influential_People "100 Most Influential People")" issue, he explained "I was interested in music and trying to find a model. It was Michael, or it was Prince. He had an attitude, a rawness that Michael didn't have. He was not urban, but he was our version of what cool could be."[[216]](#cite_note-usher1-216) Other musical influences are [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye"), [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), [Luther Vandross](/wiki/Luther_Vandross "Luther Vandross"), [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson"), [Maze featuring Frankie Beverly](/wiki/Maze_(band) "Maze (band)"), [Al Green](/wiki/Al_Green "Al Green"), and [Boyz II Men](/wiki/Boyz_II_Men "Boyz II Men").[[217]](#cite_note-217)[[218]](#cite_note-News-218)

The [boy band](/wiki/Boy_band "Boy band") [New Edition](/wiki/New_Edition "New Edition") and its solo member [Bobby Brown](/wiki/Bobby_Brown "Bobby Brown") were childhood influences. Usher told [ABC News](/wiki/ABC_News_(United_States) "ABC News (United States)"), "I can remember as a kid attending a New Edition Concert", and called it "one of the greatest moments in my performance history" years later when he was invited on stage with the group to perform ["N.E. Heartbreak"](/wiki/N.E._Heart_Break_(song) "N.E. Heart Break (song)") at a concert in [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C.")[[218]](#cite_note-News-218) On Bobby Brown, Usher told [CNN](/wiki/CNN "CNN"), "the first record that I ever bought was Bobby Brown's [*Don't Be Cruel*](/wiki/Don%27t_Be_Cruel_(album) "Don't Be Cruel (album)")", and that he admired him "Just for being an edgy artist, being a true representative of R&B and rhythmic music".[[219]](#cite_note-219) Usher's *[Live](/wiki/Live_(Usher_album) "Live (Usher album)")* album would feature a [medley](/wiki/Medley_(music) "Medley (music)") performance of Brown's original singles - "[Don't Be Cruel](/wiki/Don%27t_Be_Cruel_(Bobby_Brown_song) "Don't Be Cruel (Bobby Brown song)")", "[Every Little Step](/wiki/Every_Little_Step "Every Little Step")", "[Rock Wit'cha](/wiki/Rock_Wit%27cha "Rock Wit'cha")", and ["Roni"](/wiki/Roni_(song) "Roni (song)").
