Usher was inspired by [Frank Sinatra](/wiki/Frank_Sinatra "Frank Sinatra")'s *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Frank_Sinatra_album) "My Way (Frank Sinatra album)")* album released in 1969 so much that he decided to name his second album *My Way.*[[220]](#cite_note-220) Prince would inspire "[Nice & Slow](/wiki/Nice_%26_Slow "Nice & Slow")" while giving him his first number-one single on the [Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") chart.[[216]](#cite_note-usher1-216) Usher's third album *8701* would be inspired by love and heartache, containing influences from [Donny Hathaway](/wiki/Donny_Hathaway "Donny Hathaway"), Stevie Wonder, Marvin Gaye and Michael Jackson.[[221]](#cite_note-221)[[222]](#cite_note-222) His [fourth album](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)") was inspired by the rap group [Bone Thugs-n-Harmony](/wiki/Bone_Thugs-n-Harmony "Bone Thugs-n-Harmony"), and [Eminem](/wiki/Eminem "Eminem") whom he credited for the album having a transparent reputation. Usher said in an interview with [Sway Calloway](/wiki/Sway_Calloway "Sway Calloway"), "The honesty of hip-hop, and what Eminem was saying in his records, it motivated me. Like, yo if he could be *that* honest, you know through hip-hop - and there've been other stories, storytellers, but never quite as vivid as his...and I got that Cleveland, because, you know with the Bone Thugs-n-Harmony, you know what I'm saying, is to just understanding that the rhythm and the flip of it to make it be able to make it be at least, you know, hip-hop appropriate."[[223]](#cite_note-223)

### Vocal style

Usher possesses a [leggiero tenor](/wiki/Leggiero_tenor "Leggiero tenor") vocal range of 3 octaves and 4 notes,[[224]](#cite_note-224) and is considered to be "one of the most soothing in [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B")".[[5]](#cite_note-NT-5) At the age of 13, he set the record for the longest note ever held by a child on *[Star Search](/wiki/Star_Search "Star Search")*, with 12 seconds.[[225]](#cite_note-225) Usher's [vocal coach](/wiki/Vocal_coach "Vocal coach") since *[My Way](/wiki/My_Way_(Usher_album) "My Way (Usher album)")*, [Jan Smith](/wiki/Jan_Smith "Jan Smith"), cites him as "the single most gifted singer I've ever worked with in 26 years".[[226]](#cite_note-226) On the dynamic *[8701](/wiki/8701 "8701")*, [Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")'s Kathryn McGuire describes his vocals as "velvety" and "impressively adaptable" as it won him his first two Grammys as [Best Male R&B Vocal Performance](/wiki/Best_Male_R%26B_Vocal_Performance "Best Male R&B Vocal Performance").[[227]](#cite_note-227) With *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")*, [Sasha Frere-Jones](/wiki/Sasha_Frere-Jones "Sasha Frere-Jones") of *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* generally noted him as "long on control and short on texture".[[228]](#cite_note-newyorker1-228) He received another [Grammy Award](/wiki/Grammy_Award "Grammy Award") nomination in 2006 through a [cover](/wiki/Cover_version "Cover version") of [Luther Vandross](/wiki/Luther_Vandross "Luther Vandross")'s "[Superstar](/wiki/Superstar_(Delaney_and_Bonnie_song)#Luther_Vandross_version "Superstar (Delaney and Bonnie song)")" he performed on *[The Oprah Winfrey Show](/wiki/The_Oprah_Winfrey_Show "The Oprah Winfrey Show")* in 2005.[[229]](#cite_note-Yahoo_1-229)[[230]](#cite_note-230) *[Here I Stand](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album) "Here I Stand (Usher album)")* displayed his vocals as "agile" and "balletic",[[231]](#cite_note-231) as well as his ability to "easily drift from wavering [tenor](/wiki/Tenor "Tenor") to fine-tuned [falsetto](/wiki/Falsetto "Falsetto")" that create emotions of urgency and burning according to Clover Hope of *[The Village Voice](/wiki/The_Village_Voice "The Village Voice")*.[[232]](#cite_note-232) On his [One Night Stand: Ladies Only Tour](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album)#Tour "Here I Stand (Usher album)") in 2008, Lee Hildebrand of *[San Francisco Chronicle](/wiki/San_Francisco_Chronicle "San Francisco Chronicle")* assessed "Usher's chest tones have a ringing, [Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye")-like quality, and his high head tones suggest [Prince](/wiki/Prince_(musician) "Prince (musician)")'s falsetto, but his frequent use of syllable-splitting curlicues owes much to [Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"). Usher is a stronger, more assured singer than he was a decade ago, though the melismas in his lower range sometimes meander off pitch. His falsetto tonality is more on target."[[233]](#cite_note-SF-233)

[*Looking For Myself*](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself "Looking 4 Myself") exemplified his vocal craft earning him positive acclaim as [Gawker](/wiki/Gawker "Gawker")'s Rick Juzwiak states "his vocal range is even more impressive than his emotional one" with emphasis on his falsetto being "something that sounds as natural as a speaking voice ... he's the picture of grace against shrieking synths ... His emotional range is vast enough to sell tenderness, lechery and wistfulness within minutes of each other".[[234]](#cite_note-234) On "[Climax](/wiki/Climax_(Usher_song) "Climax (Usher song)")", his performance lauded as "blistering" by [Maura Johnston](/wiki/Maura_Johnston "Maura Johnston"),[[235]](#cite_note-235) and "hair-raising" by [Jody Rosen](/wiki/Jody_Rosen "Jody Rosen"),[[200]](#cite_note-Jody_Rosen-200) as he won [Best R&B Performance](/wiki/Best_R%26B_Performance "Best R&B Performance") in 2013.[[236]](#cite_note-236) "[There Goes My Baby](/wiki/There_Goes_My_Baby_(Usher_song) "There Goes My Baby (Usher song)")" and "[Dive](/wiki/Dive_(Usher_song) "Dive (Usher song)")" are also commended as showcase standouts of his "superior R&B vocal range" by [Consequence of Sound](/wiki/Consequence_of_Sound "Consequence of Sound")'s Ryan Hadfield,[[237]](#cite_note-237) and [MadameNoire](/wiki/MadameNoire "MadameNoire")'s Brande Victorian.[[238]](#cite_note-238) For *[Hard II Love](/wiki/Hard_II_Love "Hard II Love")*, [Jon Pareles](/wiki/Jon_Pareles "Jon Pareles") of *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* commented "a genuinely expressive voice that encompasses an ardent [croon](/wiki/Croon "Croon"), a melting falsetto and quick, singsong [declamation](/wiki/Declamation "Declamation")".[[239]](#cite_note-239) On the 2023 *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")'*s list of the 200 Greatest Singers of all time, Usher ranked at number 97.[[240]](#cite_note-240)

### Performance

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/cb/Usherraymond_%28300dpi%29.jpg/250px-Usherraymond_%28300dpi%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usherraymond_(300dpi).jpg)

Usher on his 2008 [One Night Stand: Ladies Only Tour](/wiki/Here_I_Stand_(Usher_album)#Tour "Here I Stand (Usher album)"), at the [Warfield Theatre](/wiki/Warfield_Theatre "Warfield Theatre")

Usher's stage performances have been complimented by critics as "packed with high-octane [choreography](/wiki/Choreography_(dance) "Choreography (dance)") held together by sleek, masterful showmanship",[[241]](#cite_note-241) and his presence "engaging enough to pull off a one-man show".[[229]](#cite_note-Yahoo_1-229)[[242]](#cite_note-Yahoo-242) *[Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(magazine) "Vibe (magazine)") magazine* notes him as being [Bobby Brown](/wiki/Bobby_Brown "Bobby Brown")-esque for his displays of "brash sexuality and arrogance",[[243]](#cite_note-243) leading to antics such as being "prone to dropping his pants",[[244]](#cite_note-244) and a "tendency to shed his shirt on stage",[[245]](#cite_note-Ebony-245) while John Aizlewood of *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")* observed that "For all his pelvic thrusting and crotch-grabbing, Usher is a rather courtly presence."[[246]](#cite_note-246) On his [8701 Evolution Tour](/wiki/8701_Evolution_Tour "8701 Evolution Tour") in 2002, Larry Nager of *[The Cincinnati Enquirer](/wiki/The_Cincinnati_Enquirer "The Cincinnati Enquirer")* hailed his simultaneous ability, stating "the 23-year-old Atlantan proved you can sing and dance at the same time. Of course, that's how [James Brown](/wiki/James_Brown "James Brown"), [Jackie Wilson](/wiki/Jackie_Wilson "Jackie Wilson") and Usher's most obvious role model [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") did it, but in today's high-tech, gimmicky concerts, it's a lost art", claiming his show "ended the reign of '[Nsync](/wiki/Nsync "Nsync"), [Britney](/wiki/Britney_Spears "Britney Spears") and the rest of the lip-synching, teen-pop acts".[[247]](#cite_note-247) Usher told *[People](/wiki/People_(magazine) "People (magazine)")* magazine "James Brown introduced me to soul. Because of him I was given a clear view of what a real performance is and should be. I learned showmanship from him",[[248]](#cite_note-248) and has seen comparisons to his influences.[[242]](#cite_note-Yahoo-242) At the [2004 MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/2004_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2004 MTV Video Music Awards"), he performed "[Confessions Part II](/wiki/Confessions_Part_II "Confessions Part II")" and "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")" under a rain machine, in which he eventually threw the [mic stand](/wiki/Mic_stand "Mic stand") at a mirror during the former. *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* placed it at number 13 of their "20 Best [MTV VMAs](/wiki/MTV_VMAs "MTV VMAs") Opening Performances".[[249]](#cite_note-249)

> Usher is an old-school showman who has been performing since childhood: a dancer, a singer, five feet and seven inches of raw charm and taut musculature. I've seen superstars perform before, but always in large venues, where they are isolated from the plebes by an appropriate distance. Seeing Usher in such a small venue, beaming ear-to-ear the whole evening, I was bowled over by the sheer amount of raw talent and charisma on display.

—Anna Silman of *[New York Magazine](/wiki/New_York_Magazine "New York Magazine")*[[250]](#cite_note-250)

Chad Batka for *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* said "He has established himself as one of R&B's supreme performers and craftsmen, following through on what he learned from Michael Jackson in particular: songs that alternate between choppy and smooth, vocals punctuated by exultant high whoops, a dance vocabulary."[[201]](#cite_note-NY-201) Capable of playing the [piano](/wiki/Piano "Piano"),[[251]](#cite_note-Fusion-251)[[252]](#cite_note-252) he also began to adopt playing on other instruments such as the [drums](/wiki/Drums "Drums") and [bass guitar](/wiki/Bass_guitar "Bass guitar"), amid singles "[Good Kisser](/wiki/Good_Kisser "Good Kisser")" and "[She Came to Give It to You](/wiki/She_Came_to_Give_It_to_You "She Came to Give It to You")" in 2014.[[242]](#cite_note-Yahoo-242)[[253]](#cite_note-253) The [UR Experience Tour](/wiki/UR_Experience_Tour "UR Experience Tour") consisted of a nine-piece [band](/wiki/Musical_ensemble "Musical ensemble") and three [backing vocalists](/wiki/Backing_vocalist "Backing vocalist") according to [Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)"),[[254]](#cite_note-254) as *The Guardian* called him "a masterful all-rounder who surrounds himself with talent on stage".[[255]](#cite_note-255) [Maura Johnston](/wiki/Maura_Johnston "Maura Johnston") of *Rolling Stone* labels him the "Award Show King", explaining "His dedication to choreography dates back to the days when [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson") made videos that looked like they were shot in one take. He's an entertainer that would have been stunning at any point in the last 60 years of television."[[256]](#cite_note-256)

## Public image

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2b/Usher_on_USS_Kearsarge_2_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Usher_on_USS_Kearsarge_2_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_on_USS_Kearsarge_2_(cropped).jpg)

Usher aboard the [USS *Kearsarge*](/wiki/USS_Kearsarge_(LHD-3) "USS Kearsarge (LHD-3)") in 2008

Usher has been regarded as "an international [sex symbol](/wiki/Sex_symbol "Sex symbol")" since a young age, predominantly for his [stage persona](/wiki/Persona#In_music "Persona").[[245]](#cite_note-Ebony-245) [Fusion](/wiki/Fusion_(TV_channel) "Fusion (TV channel)") states "Usher—one of the greatest R&B sex symbols in the past 20 years, paving the way for [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"), [Trey Songz](/wiki/Trey_Songz "Trey Songz") and [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber")—used sex as a major part of his image",[[251]](#cite_note-Fusion-251) while [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1") considers him to be "the sexiest male performer ever to walk a stage".[[257]](#cite_note-257) [*People*](/wiki/People_(magazine) "People (magazine)") magazine named Usher the "Sexiest R&B Star" in 1998, with singer [Monica](/wiki/Monica_(singer) "Monica (singer)") who said on stage sexiness was manifest in "his movements, his gestures, the way he expresses his lyrics".[[258]](#cite_note-258) They included him in their 2004 "Sexiest Men Alive",[[259]](#cite_note-259) and 2008 "Sexiest Man Alive" lists.[[260]](#cite_note-260) *[The New York Times](/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")* writer [Kelefa Sanneh](/wiki/Kelefa_Sanneh "Kelefa Sanneh") referred to him as "one of the country's most famous ex-boyfriends" after his breakup with [Chilli](/wiki/Rozonda_Thomas "Rozonda Thomas") of [TLC](/wiki/TLC_(group) "TLC (group)").[[261]](#cite_note-261) He was ranked number-one on [BET](/wiki/BET "BET")'s "All Shades of Fine: 25 Hottest Men of the Past 25 Years" special in 2005.[[262]](#cite_note-262)[[263]](#cite_note-263) [Madame Tussauds](/wiki/Madame_Tussauds "Madame Tussauds") New York also unveiled a [wax figure](/wiki/Wax_sculpture "Wax sculpture") of Usher during a competition to launch The Usher Interactive Experience,[[264]](#cite_note-264) and for the opening of a VIP room in 2007.[[265]](#cite_note-265) *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* magazine ranks "[Confessions Part II](/wiki/Confessions_Part_II "Confessions Part II")" amongst its "30 Sexiest Music Videos of All Time".[[266]](#cite_note-266)

After his marriage to [Tameka Foster](/wiki/Tameka_Foster "Tameka Foster"), his sexual appeal was expected to mature as *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* writer [Sasha Frere-Jones](/wiki/Sasha_Frere-Jones "Sasha Frere-Jones") wrote "The challenge here is to convince us that he is a married and responsible man—grown and sexy, [...]—without sacrificing the louche, frictionless sense of play that made him famous."[[228]](#cite_note-newyorker1-228) [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1") have listed Usher at number 9 of its 100 Sexiest Artists List.[[267]](#cite_note-267) According to [*Glamour* magazine](/wiki/Glamour_(magazine) "Glamour (magazine)"), he was one of the "Sexiest Men" of 2010,[[268]](#cite_note-268) and 2011.[[269]](#cite_note-269) In 2012, [Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)") reviewed his fashion evolution stating "The singer came onto the scene in the '90s rocking [baggy jeans](/wiki/Baggy_jeans "Baggy jeans") and suits that made us question his celebrity status, but over the years this R&B star has developed his own signature look, rendering him a modern day fashion icon".[[270]](#cite_note-270) In 2013, [Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)") placed him in its "Most Stylish R&B Singers of the 90s" list.[[271]](#cite_note-271) *[Men's Health](/wiki/Men%27s_Health "Men's Health")* magazine was covered by Usher on its 25th anniversary issue, with [Lou Schuler](/wiki/Lou_Schuler "Lou Schuler") calling him "the man with the best abs in the entertainment industry".[[272]](#cite_note-272) In 2014 and 2015, his [Calvin Klein Collection](/wiki/Calvin_Klein_Collection "Calvin Klein Collection") suits saw him listed as "Best Dressed Men" by outlets at the [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/71st_Golden_Globe_Awards "71st Golden Globe Awards")[[273]](#cite_note-273)[[274]](#cite_note-274)[[275]](#cite_note-275) and [Grammy Awards](/wiki/57th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "57th Annual Grammy Awards").[[276]](#cite_note-276)[[277]](#cite_note-277)[[278]](#cite_note-278) [InStyle](/wiki/InStyle "InStyle") have rated him both in their "Hottest Celebrity Abs" collection,[[279]](#cite_note-279) and "37 of the Most Stylish Dads" list.[[280]](#cite_note-280)

## Legacy

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/American_Artist_Usher_Delivers_Remarks_at_the_2015_Kennedy_Center_Honors_Dinner_in_Washington_%2823586745516%29.jpg/330px-American_Artist_Usher_Delivers_Remarks_at_the_2015_Kennedy_Center_Honors_Dinner_in_Washington_%2823586745516%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:American_Artist_Usher_Delivers_Remarks_at_the_2015_Kennedy_Center_Honors_Dinner_in_Washington_(23586745516).jpg)

Usher at the 2015 [Kennedy Center Honors](/wiki/Kennedy_Center_Honors "Kennedy Center Honors")

Usher was named the [Hot 100](/wiki/Billboard_Hot_100 "Billboard Hot 100") Artist of the 2000s by *[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")* stating "Usher's sexy brand of R&B dominated the last decade with the top-selling album and more No. 1s than any other act",[[281]](#cite_note-281)[[282]](#cite_note-282) with *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* selling 20 million copies worldwide as the top solo album.[[283]](#cite_note-283) Considered by Bonsu Thompson of [Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(magazine) "Vibe (magazine)") to be "the *[Thriller](/wiki/Thriller_(Michael_Jackson_album) "Thriller (Michael Jackson album)")* of our generation",[[284]](#cite_note-284) Kia Turner of *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* stated that it "became ingrained in [pop culture](/wiki/Pop_culture "Pop culture")" because of its theme.[[285]](#cite_note-R.S-285) Pop music critic [Sasha Frere-Jones](/wiki/Sasha_Frere-Jones "Sasha Frere-Jones") of *[The New Yorker](/wiki/The_New_Yorker "The New Yorker")* remarks that "For a while, *Confessions* was pop music" and says it "may turn out to have been the last true [blockbuster](/wiki/Blockbuster_(entertainment) "Blockbuster (entertainment)") in pop".[[228]](#cite_note-newyorker1-228) [Clay Cane](/wiki/Clay_Cane "Clay Cane") said in 2008 "We didn't see pop-R&B artists on [pop radio](/wiki/Pop_radio "Pop radio"), and Usher is one of those people in the 90s who was the catalyst for that; he was kind of the pioneer ... He's the greatest example of pure [crossover](/wiki/Crossover_music "Crossover music") success".[[286]](#cite_note-286) Described as the best dancer in pop since [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") by writers of *[VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1")*[[287]](#cite_note-287) and *[The Guardian](/wiki/The_Guardian "The Guardian")*,[[199]](#cite_note-Usher-199) [Jody Rosen](/wiki/Jody_Rosen "Jody Rosen") said in 2012 "He's the biggest male pop singer in the world; sometimes, it seems like he's the only one, in a marketplace still dominated by [divas](/wiki/Divas "Divas")".[[200]](#cite_note-Jody_Rosen-200) Attaining 9 *Billboard* Hot 100 number-one singles to tie with the [Bee Gees](/wiki/Bee_Gees "Bee Gees"), [Elton John](/wiki/Elton_John "Elton John"), and [Paul McCartney](/wiki/Paul_McCartney "Paul McCartney"),[[288]](#cite_note-9HOT100-288) Justin Charity of *[Complex](/wiki/Complex_(magazine) "Complex (magazine)")* referred to him as "the greatest male pop singer of the past couple decades" in 2015.[[289]](#cite_note-289)

[James Brown](/wiki/James_Brown "James Brown"), performed a duet with Usher in 2005 at the [47th Grammy Awards](/wiki/47th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "47th Annual Grammy Awards") in which he honorably dubbed him the Godson of Soul.[[290]](#cite_note-290) Brown reiterated "The new godson; Usher, the new godson" following the performance.[[291]](#cite_note-291) Often cited as an "[icon](/wiki/Pop_icon "Pop icon")",[[292]](#cite_note-292)[[293]](#cite_note-293)[[294]](#cite_note-294)[[295]](#cite_note-295) *[Los Angeles Times](/wiki/Los_Angeles_Times "Los Angeles Times")* critic Randall Roberts marks dance singles "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")", "[Caught Up](/wiki/Caught_Up_(Usher_song) "Caught Up (Usher song)")" and "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")" to have influenced contemporary [dance-pop](/wiki/Dance-pop#2010s "Dance-pop"), stating the former two also "helped define the [20]00s".[[210]](#cite_note-LA_Times-210) *Time* magazine's pop stardom ranking metric, ranked Usher 3rd in history, based on "all-time chart performance and contemporary significance".[[296]](#cite_note-296) *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* magazine named "[Confessions Part II](/wiki/Confessions_Part_II "Confessions Part II")" the greatest R&B song of the 21st century and "[U Don't Have to Call](/wiki/U_Don%27t_Have_to_Call "U Don't Have to Call")" the 25th.[[285]](#cite_note-R.S-285)

Usher has been referred to as the "King of R&B",[[297]](#cite_note-297) as well as "Mr. Entertainment"[[298]](#cite_note-298) by *The Washington Times* and *Chicago Tribune*.[[299]](#cite_note-299)[[300]](#cite_note-300) [The BET Honors](/wiki/The_BET_Honors "The BET Honors") honored him for his contributions to music with a Musical Arts award in 2015, presented by [Bobby Brown](/wiki/Bobby_Brown "Bobby Brown") who said "You remain a game changer, for your discipline, for your perfectionism. You're just a god man".[[301]](#cite_note-301)[[302]](#cite_note-302) The BET Honors highlighted his influence on many contemporary artists such as [Chris Brown](/wiki/Chris_Brown "Chris Brown"),[[303]](#cite_note-303) [Trey Songz](/wiki/Trey_Songz "Trey Songz"),[[304]](#cite_note-304) [Drake](/wiki/Drake_(musician) "Drake (musician)"),[[305]](#cite_note-305) [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"),[[306]](#cite_note-BET-306) [Jason Derulo](/wiki/Jason_Derulo "Jason Derulo"),[[307]](#cite_note-307) [Miguel](/wiki/Miguel_(singer) "Miguel (singer)"),[[306]](#cite_note-BET-306) [Omarion](/wiki/Omarion "Omarion"),[[308]](#cite_note-308) [Ray J](/wiki/Ray_J "Ray J"),[[309]](#cite_note-309) [August Alsina](/wiki/August_Alsina "August Alsina"),[[310]](#cite_note-310) and [Eric Bellinger](/wiki/Eric_Bellinger "Eric Bellinger").[[311]](#cite_note-311) [Ne-Yo](/wiki/Ne-Yo "Ne-Yo") further cited Usher as an influence upon the honor saying "I feel that without Usher, there would be no me, there would be no Chris Brown, there would be no Trey Songz. He paved the way for us".[[312]](#cite_note-312) Other artists who have also cited him as an influence are [Zayn Malik](/wiki/Zayn_Malik "Zayn Malik") and [Liam Payne](/wiki/Liam_Payne "Liam Payne") of [One Direction](/wiki/One_Direction "One Direction"),[[313]](#cite_note-313) [Bryson Tiller](/wiki/Bryson_Tiller "Bryson Tiller"),[[314]](#cite_note-314) [Jungkook](/wiki/Jungkook "Jungkook") of [BTS](/wiki/BTS "BTS"),[[315]](#cite_note-315) [Lucky Daye](/wiki/Lucky_Daye "Lucky Daye"),[[316]](#cite_note-316) [6lack](/wiki/6lack "6lack"),[[317]](#cite_note-317) [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love"),[[318]](#cite_note-318) [Jeremih](/wiki/Jeremih "Jeremih"),[[319]](#cite_note-319) [Anderson Paak](/wiki/Anderson_Paak "Anderson Paak"),[[320]](#cite_note-320) [Trevor Jackson](/wiki/Trevor_Jackson_(performer) "Trevor Jackson (performer)"),[[321]](#cite_note-321) [Sam Hunt](/wiki/Sam_Hunt "Sam Hunt"),[[322]](#cite_note-322) [Leon Bridges](/wiki/Leon_Bridges "Leon Bridges"),[[323]](#cite_note-323) [Sammie](/wiki/Sammie "Sammie"),[[324]](#cite_note-324) [B. Smyth](/wiki/B._Smyth "B. Smyth"),[[325]](#cite_note-325) [Jacob Latimore](/wiki/Jacob_Latimore "Jacob Latimore"),[[326]](#cite_note-326) [Kevin Woo](/wiki/Kevin_Woo "Kevin Woo"),[[327]](#cite_note-327) [Keke Palmer](/wiki/Keke_Palmer "Keke Palmer"),[[328]](#cite_note-328) [Justine Skye](/wiki/Justine_Skye "Justine Skye"),[[329]](#cite_note-329) [BJ The Chicago Kid](/wiki/BJ_the_Chicago_Kid "BJ the Chicago Kid"),[[330]](#cite_note-330) [Roy Woods](/wiki/Roy_Woods "Roy Woods"),[[331]](#cite_note-331) [YK Osiris](/wiki/YK_Osiris "YK Osiris"),[[332]](#cite_note-332) [Lukas Magyar](/wiki/Veil_of_Maya "Veil of Maya") of [Veil of Maya](/wiki/Veil_of_Maya "Veil of Maya"),[[333]](#cite_note-333) [Bruno Mars](/wiki/Bruno_Mars "Bruno Mars"),[[334]](#cite_note-334) and actor [Tahj Mowry](/wiki/Tahj_Mowry "Tahj Mowry").[[335]](#cite_note-335)

## Achievements

See also: [List of awards and nominations received by Usher](/wiki/List_of_awards_and_nominations_received_by_Usher "List of awards and nominations received by Usher")

Usher has received numerous awards throughout his career from having the 14× platinum Diamond certified album *Confessions*, four multi-platinum albums in *My Way*, *8701*, *Raymond v. Raymond* and *Here I Stand* and one platinum album in *Looking 4 Myself*, collectively selling over 33 million albums in the US and 53 million albums worldwide.[[336]](#cite_note-336)[[33]](#cite_note-Certifications-33) This includes 8 [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), 18 [Billboard Music Awards](/wiki/Billboard_Music_Award "Billboard Music Award"), 12 [Soul Train Music Awards](/wiki/Soul_Train_Music_Awards "Soul Train Music Awards") and 8 [American Music Awards](/wiki/American_Music_Award "American Music Award").[[337]](#cite_note-337) In [2004](/wiki/2004_Billboard_Music_Awards "2004 Billboard Music Awards"), Usher became the top Billboard honoree by taking 11 awards.[[338]](#cite_note-338) At the 26th annual [American Society of Composers, Authors and Publishers](/wiki/American_Society_of_Composers,_Authors_and_Publishers "American Society of Composers, Authors and Publishers") (ASCAP) Rhythm & Soul Music Awards in 2013, he was honored with the Golden Note Award.[[339]](#cite_note-339) [Yahoo Music](/wiki/Yahoo_Music "Yahoo Music") classed him as the best [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") act of the decade behind [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") on album sales,[[340]](#cite_note-340) while *[Fuse](/wiki/Fuse_(magazine) "Fuse (magazine)")* ranked him as the 10th most award-winning musician of all time.[[341]](#cite_note-341) He has sold over a total of 100 million records.[[342]](#cite_note-342) Usher was inducted into the 29th Annual [Georgia Music Hall of Fame](/wiki/Georgia_Music_Hall_of_Fame "Georgia Music Hall of Fame") in 2007,[[343]](#cite_note-343) and the [Hollywood Walk of Fame](/wiki/Hollywood_Walk_of_Fame "Hollywood Walk of Fame") in 2016 with a star located at 6201 Hollywood Boulevard.[[344]](#cite_note-344) On June 17, 2021, Usher was inducted into the [Black Music & Entertainment Walk of Fame](/wiki/Black_Music_%26_Entertainment_Walk_of_Fame "Black Music & Entertainment Walk of Fame") as a member of the inaugural class.[[345]](#cite_note-345) His songs "Love in This Club" and "OMG" are also amongst the [best-selling digital singles](/wiki/List_of_best-selling_singles#Best-selling_digital_singles "List of best-selling singles") worldwide.[[346]](#cite_note-346)[[347]](#cite_note-347)

Usher amassed nine number-one and eighteen top-ten singles on the *Billboard* Hot 100,[[348]](#cite_note-348) and accumulated 47 weeks at the top, more than any other male solo artist at the time.[[349]](#cite_note-349) He spent 28 weeks at number-one in 2004 alone, marking a then-record for most cumulative weeks spent atop in a calendar year on the Hot 100 of all time.[[350]](#cite_note-350) "OMG" made him the first 2010s artist to collect number-one singles in three consecutive decades (1990s, 2000s, 2010s).[[351]](#cite_note-351) He became the fifth artist of all-time to achieve that feat, following [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson"), [Janet Jackson](/wiki/Janet_Jackson "Janet Jackson"), and [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna"),[[352]](#cite_note-USHERINGIN-352) and became the third male soloist in history to have at least one number-one single from five consecutive studio albums.[[288]](#cite_note-9HOT100-288) On the [Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Songs "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Songs") chart, Usher has 13 number-one singles tied with [Michael Jackson](/wiki/Michael_Jackson "Michael Jackson") and [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye"),[[353]](#cite_note-353) the most of the 2000s with 8 number-ones,[[354]](#cite_note-354) and set a chart *[Guinness World Record](/wiki/Guinness_World_Record "Guinness World Record")* in 2010.[[355]](#cite_note-355) Usher also has 14 number-one singles on the [Rhythmic](/wiki/Rhythmic_(chart) "Rhythmic (chart)") chart,[[356]](#cite_note-356) 17 number-one singles on the [Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop Songs](/wiki/Mainstream_R%26B/Hip-Hop "Mainstream R&B/Hip-Hop") chart,[[182]](#cite_note-Rutherford-182) and 16 number-one singles on the [Airplay](/wiki/Hot_R%26B/Hip-Hop_Airplay "Hot R&B/Hip-Hop Airplay") chart,[[357]](#cite_note-ReferenceB-357)[[358]](#cite_note-358) with "You Make Me Wanna..." (12 weeks) and "[Climax](/wiki/Climax_(Usher_song) "Climax (Usher song)")" (11 weeks) as two of the longest stays on the latter.[[359]](#cite_note-359)[[360]](#cite_note-360) Usher has topped several [Billboard Year-End](/wiki/Billboard_Year-End "Billboard Year-End") charts including seven in 1998,[[361]](#cite_note-361) seventeen in 2004,[[362]](#cite_note-362) and one in 2010.[[363]](#cite_note-363) The Year-End Hot 100 chart of 2004 was topped by "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")" while "[Burn](/wiki/Burn_(Usher_song) "Burn (Usher song)")" held second place,[[364]](#cite_note-364) making him the first act since [The Beatles](/wiki/The_Beatles "The Beatles") in 1964 to have two singles occupy the top two positions.[[365]](#cite_note-365) On the 2000s Billboard Decade-End chart, "Yeah!" finished second, behind [Mariah Carey](/wiki/Mariah_Carey "Mariah Carey")'s "[We Belong Together](/wiki/We_Belong_Together "We Belong Together")".[[366]](#cite_note-366)

*[Billboard](/wiki/Billboard_(magazine) "Billboard (magazine)")'*s 2nd most successful artist of the 2000s,[[367]](#cite_note-367) he was placed at number 6 on their list of "Top 50 R&B/Hip-Hop Artists of the Past 25 Years".[[368]](#cite_note-368) The same magazine placed him at number 14 on their "Greatest of All Time Hot 100 Artists" list,[[369]](#cite_note-GOATH100-369) 6 on their "Top 60 Male Artists of All-Time" list,[[370]](#cite_note-370) and 31 on their "35 Greatest [R&B](/wiki/Contemporary_R%26B "Contemporary R&B") Artists of All Time" list.[[371]](#cite_note-371) *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")* is placed at number 16 on their "Greatest of All Time 200 Albums" list,[[372]](#cite_note-372) and 10 on *[Vibe](/wiki/Vibe_(magazine) "Vibe (magazine)")* magazine's "Greatest 50 Albums since '93".[[373]](#cite_note-373) "[Yeah!](/wiki/Yeah!_(Usher_song) "Yeah! (Usher song)")" is placed at number 14 in their "Greatest of All Time Hot 100 Songs" list,[[374]](#cite_note-374) and 15 on [VH1](/wiki/VH1 "VH1")'s "Greatest Songs of the 2000s".[[375]](#cite_note-375)

## Business and other ventures

### Music

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f0/Usher_Raymond_by_Sandra_Alphonse_2.jpg/250px-Usher_Raymond_by_Sandra_Alphonse_2.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_Raymond_by_Sandra_Alphonse_2.jpg)

Usher at [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love")'s [Division 1](/wiki/Rico_Love#Division_1 "Rico Love") launch in 2010

Usher founded [vanity](/wiki/Vanity_label "Vanity label") record label US Records in 2002. The label was a subsidiary of [Clive Davis](/wiki/Clive_Davis "Clive Davis")'s J Records, which was distributed by [Sony BMG](/wiki/Sony_BMG "Sony BMG"). The only album released by US Records was the soundtrack to *In the Mix* in late 2005, which was used to introduce the label's acts, such as rapper [Rico Love](/wiki/Rico_Love "Rico Love"), singer Rayan, Canadian teen R&B artist [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"), and [One Chance](/wiki/One_Chance_(group) "One Chance (group)"), an R&B vocal quartet composed of Jon A. Gordon, Michael A. Gordon, Courtney Vantrease, and Robert Brent. Usher served as songwriter-producer in the project.[[376]](#cite_note-376)[[377]](#cite_note-377) The [Raymond Braun Media Group](/wiki/RBMG_Records "RBMG Records"), which [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber") is signed up to, is a joint venture between Usher and Bieber's manager [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun").[[378]](#cite_note-378) In 2023, Usher and longtime friend L.A. Reid co-founded Mega, an independent record label in a partnership with Larry Jackson's digital music distribution company, Gamma.[[379]](#cite_note-379)

Usher served as the contestant mentor for the Top 10 Week of Season 9 of the television show [American Idol](/wiki/American_Idol "American Idol").[[380]](#cite_note-380) He appeared on the [ITV1](/wiki/ITV1 "ITV1") show [Britain's Got Talent](/wiki/Britain%27s_Got_Talent "Britain's Got Talent") on June 5, 2010. Usher joined as a new coach on the [NBC](/wiki/NBC "NBC") American reality talent show [The Voice](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._TV_series) "The Voice (U.S. TV series)"), alongside [Adam Levine](/wiki/Adam_Levine "Adam Levine"), [Blake Shelton](/wiki/Blake_Shelton "Blake Shelton"), and [Shakira](/wiki/Shakira "Shakira") in [season 4](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._season_4) "The Voice (U.S. season 4)"), which premiered on March 25, 2013.[[381]](#cite_note-381) Usher reached the finale with contestant [Michelle Chamuel](/wiki/Michelle_Chamuel "Michelle Chamuel") on June 13, 2013.[[382]](#cite_note-382) Usher returned with Shakira in [season 6](/wiki/The_Voice_(U.S._season_6) "The Voice (U.S. season 6)") of The Voice, which premiered on February 24, 2014.[[383]](#cite_note-383) Usher reached the finale again, and won with contestant [Josh Kaufman](/wiki/Josh_Kaufman_(singer) "Josh Kaufman (singer)") on May 20, 2014.[[384]](#cite_note-384)

### Acting

Usher made his television acting debut on the American [sitcom](/wiki/Sitcom "Sitcom") *[Moesha](/wiki/Moesha "Moesha")* in 1997 playing Jeremy Davis, the love interest and classmate of Moesha, played by [Brandy Norwood](/wiki/Brandy_Norwood "Brandy Norwood").[[385]](#cite_note-385) He has acted in feature films, debuting in 1998's *[The Faculty](/wiki/The_Faculty "The Faculty")*. In 1999, Usher appeared as a student disc jockey in the teen romantic comedy film *[She's All That](/wiki/She%27s_All_That "She's All That")*. The same year he starred in his first leading role in the movie *[Light It Up](/wiki/Light_It_Up_(film) "Light It Up (film)")* with [Rosario Dawson](/wiki/Rosario_Dawson "Rosario Dawson"), [Vanessa L. Williams](/wiki/Vanessa_L._Williams "Vanessa L. Williams"), and [Forest Whitaker](/wiki/Forest_Whitaker "Forest Whitaker"). The following year, he played in *[Gepetto](/wiki/Geppetto_(film) "Geppetto (film)")* and *[Texas Rangers](/wiki/Texas_Rangers_(film) "Texas Rangers (film)")*. He starred in the [2002 *Twilight Zone* revival](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone_(2002_TV_series) "The Twilight Zone (2002 TV series)") episode [To Protect and Serve](/wiki/To_Protect_and_Serve_(The_Twilight_Zone) "To Protect and Serve (The Twilight Zone)") as a police officer. Usher continued to make appearances in various television shows, including the period drama *[American Dreams](/wiki/American_Dreams "American Dreams")* (2002) in the role of [Marvin Gaye](/wiki/Marvin_Gaye "Marvin Gaye"). He also appeared in *[Sabrina the Teenage Witch](/wiki/Sabrina_the_Teenage_Witch_(1996_TV_series) "Sabrina the Teenage Witch (1996 TV series)")* in [Season 2, episode 6](/wiki/List_of_Sabrina_the_Teenage_Witch_episodes#Season_2_(1997–1998) "List of Sabrina the Teenage Witch episodes") playing the role of a love doctor.[[386]](#cite_note-386) In 2005, he starred in the [crime](/wiki/Crime "Crime")-[comedy](/wiki/Comedy "Comedy") film [*In The Mix*](/wiki/In_the_Mix_(film) "In the Mix (film)") with [Chazz Palminteri](/wiki/Chazz_Palminteri "Chazz Palminteri") and [Kevin Hart](/wiki/Kevin_Hart "Kevin Hart"). He has also acted on stage, making his debut on [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") in 2006 in *[Chicago](/wiki/Chicago_(play) "Chicago (play)")* as lawyer Billy Flynn and joining the cast of the [Off-Broadway](/wiki/Off-Broadway "Off-Broadway") play *[Fuerzabruta](/wiki/Fuerzabruta "Fuerzabruta")* in 2012;[[387]](#cite_note-387) the former performance was critiqued by theater critic [Ben Brantley](/wiki/Ben_Brantley "Ben Brantley") as unfitting for the character played stating "Usher was "cast so hard against type, you would think you would hear him cracking from the collision", despite commending his vocal and choreographic performance in the show.[[388]](#cite_note-388) In 2013, he began filming his role as boxer [Sugar Ray Leonard](/wiki/Sugar_Ray_Leonard "Sugar Ray Leonard") for the 2016 American [biographical film](/wiki/Biographical_film "Biographical film") *[Hands of Stone](/wiki/Hands_of_Stone "Hands of Stone")*, which follows the life of Panamanian boxer [Roberto Durán](/wiki/Roberto_Dur%C3%A1n "Roberto Durán"). In the film he stars alongside [Édgar Ramírez](/wiki/%C3%89dgar_Ram%C3%ADrez "Édgar Ramírez"), [Robert De Niro](/wiki/Robert_De_Niro "Robert De Niro"), [Ellen Barkin](/wiki/Ellen_Barkin "Ellen Barkin"), and [Ana de Armas](/wiki/Ana_de_Armas "Ana de Armas"). The film debuted at the [Cannes Film Festival](/wiki/Cannes_Film_Festival "Cannes Film Festival") in [Cannes, France](/wiki/Cannes "Cannes") on May 16, 2016, and was released in the United States on August 26, 2016.[[389]](#cite_note-389)

### Products and services

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/65/Usher_Ring_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Usher_Ring_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_Ring_(cropped).jpg)

Usher at his 2007 launch of fragrances *Usher He* and *Usher She*

Aside from recording music, Usher is involved in other businesses, including several restaurants.[[390]](#cite_note-390)[[391]](#cite_note-391) In 2007, Usher worked with Richard Herpin and Honoring Blanc for the fragrances *[Usher He](/wiki/Usher_(fragrance) "Usher (fragrance)")* and *[Usher She](/wiki/Usher_(fragrance) "Usher (fragrance)")*. The promotion of the fragrance featured Usher and [Martha Stewart](/wiki/Martha_Stewart "Martha Stewart") in the [Macy's](/wiki/Macy%27s "Macy's") "The Magic of Macy's" commercial.[[392]](#cite_note-392) In September 2008, Usher released the fragrances *UR for Men* and *UR for Women*. Usher's fifth fragrance, *[VIP](/wiki/Usher_VIP "Usher VIP")*, was launched in 2009.[[393]](#cite_note-393)

On March 30, 2015, it was announced that Usher is a co-owner, with various other music artists, in the music streaming service [Tidal](/wiki/Tidal_(service) "Tidal (service)"). The service specializes in lossless audio and high definition music video with curated editorial. [Jay Z](/wiki/Jay_Z "Jay Z") acquired the parent company of Tidal, [Aspiro](/wiki/Aspiro "Aspiro"), in the first quarter of 2015. The slogan "Turn the tide" and the hashtag "#TIDALforAll" was used by various artists on social media during the buildup to the press conference relaunching the service.[[394]](#cite_note-394) Usher, along with sixteen artist stakeholders (such as [Alicia Keys](/wiki/Alicia_Keys "Alicia Keys"), [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Beyoncé](/wiki/Beyonc%C3%A9 "Beyoncé"), [Rihanna](/wiki/Rihanna "Rihanna"), [Madonna](/wiki/Madonna "Madonna"), [J.Cole](/wiki/J.Cole_(rapper) "J.Cole (rapper)"), [Nicki Minaj](/wiki/Nicki_Minaj "Nicki Minaj"), and more) co-own Tidal, with the majority owning a 3% equity stake.[[395]](#cite_note-395) The idea of having an all artist owned streaming service was created by those involved to adapt to the increased demand for streaming within the current music industry, and to rival other streaming services such as [Spotify](/wiki/Spotify "Spotify"), which have been criticized for their low payout of royalties. Jay-Z stated "The challenge is to get everyone to respect music again, to recognize its value".[[396]](#cite_note-396)

On June 28, 2015, Usher became partners with Yoobi the fast-growing school supplies company called based out of [Los Angeles](/wiki/Los_Angeles "Los Angeles").[[397]](#cite_note-397) The company donates a portion of its products to schools and has brought in over $20 million in revenue between June 2014 and June 2015, Yoobi CEO [Ido Leffler](/wiki/Ido_Leffler "Ido Leffler") told *[Inc.](/wiki/Inc._(magazine) "Inc. (magazine)")* The Usher line of supplies with Yoobi features a collaboration with [Jonni Cheatwood](/wiki/Jonni_Cheatwood "Jonni Cheatwood"), the artist who does the designing. The slogan is "Mixing street smarts with school smarts". The collection is available online and at [Target Corporation](/wiki/Target_Corporation "Target Corporation") stores nationwide, and for every purchase, school supplies are donated to kids in need in US classrooms. When asked why he partnered with Yoobi, Usher said, "Preparing future leaders through my [New Look Foundation](/wiki/Usher%27s_New_Look "Usher's New Look") has always been about having the proper tools to succeed in life. If they don't have the tools, young men and women from underserved communities can't even begin to understand the idea of having a new or different perspective. So that's where this collaboration came together giving young people the tools and supplying them with hope."[[398]](#cite_note-398)

### Investments

Usher is a part owner of the [Cleveland Cavaliers](/wiki/Cleveland_Cavaliers "Cleveland Cavaliers") professional basketball team. He is part of a group which bought the team with a reported total purchase price of $375 million in 2005. He became the third pop artist to own a large stake in an [National Basketball Association](/wiki/National_Basketball_Association "National Basketball Association") (NBA) team.[[399]](#cite_note-399) During his tenure, the Cleveland Cavaliers have won five Central Division Championships, three Eastern Conference Champions and the [2016 NBA championship](/wiki/2016_NBA_Finals "2016 NBA Finals") on June 19, 2016, which he won as a minority owner.[[400]](#cite_note-400)

On March 6, 2017, it was announced that [Universal Music Group](/wiki/Universal_Music_Group "Universal Music Group"), Evolution Media, [Jon Jashni](/wiki/Jon_Jashni "Jon Jashni"), Usher, [Steve Stoute](/wiki/Steve_Stoute "Steve Stoute"), Charles King, and others invested $6 Million in [Mass Appeal](/wiki/Mass_Appeal_(media) "Mass Appeal (media)").[[401]](#cite_note-401) Mass Appeal is a New York City based media and content company. In 1996, Mass Appeal was founded as a graffiti print magazine. In 2014, Mass Appeal relaunched after CEO Peter Bittenbender partnered with American rapper [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas"), who would serve as the publication's associate publisher.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On April 2, 2019, Usher, [Jay-Z](/wiki/Jay-Z "Jay-Z"), [Walter Robb](/wiki/Walter_Robb "Walter Robb"), [Seth Goldman](/wiki/Seth_Goldman_(businessman) "Seth Goldman (businessman)"), [Ndamukong Suh](/wiki/Ndamukong_Suh "Ndamukong Suh"), and others invested $8 Million in a catering startup called Hungry.[[402]](#cite_note-402) Hungry is a service that connects local chefs with companies looking for business and event catering. The catering company partnered with J's Kitchen Culinary Incubator, a business owned by Usher's mother Jonetta Patton.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

On July 23, 2019, it was announced Usher, [Kevin Hart](/wiki/Kevin_Hart "Kevin Hart"), [Justin Bieber](/wiki/Justin_Bieber "Justin Bieber"), [Demi Lovato](/wiki/Demi_Lovato "Demi Lovato"), [DJ Khaled](/wiki/DJ_Khaled "DJ Khaled"), [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun"), and [Kendall Jenner](/wiki/Kendall_Jenner "Kendall Jenner") among others collectively invested $5 Million in Liquid I.V., a health-science and nutrition wellness company based out of [El Segundo, California](/wiki/El_Segundo,_California "El Segundo, California").[[403]](#cite_note-403) The company was founded in 2012 by Brandin Cohen to help children in developing countries suffering from extreme dehydration caused by deadly diseases.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

### Endorsements

Usher has worked with [MasterCard](/wiki/MasterCard "MasterCard") since 2004. Together, they launched the Usher Debit MasterCard, a sleek black card that features Usher's face and was handed out to concertgoers during his *[The Truth Tour](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")*.[[404]](#cite_note-404) In 2009, he partnered with [Belvedere](/wiki/Belvedere_(vodka) "Belvedere (vodka)") Vodka, launching the campaign Product (Red) that went to donate half of all profits from a new special-edition bottle to fight HIV/AIDS in Africa.[[405]](#cite_note-405) MasterCard again teamed up with Usher in 2015 to surprise one cardholder. Usher was disguised as a street performer to surprise one of his biggest British fans, who was flown into Los Angeles as part of MasterCard's Priceless Surprises campaign in support of the 2015 BRIT Awards.[[406]](#cite_note-406)

In 2012, Usher partnered with the number-one selling dance game [Microsoft](/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft") [Dance Central 3](/wiki/Dance_Central_3 "Dance Central 3"). Exclusively made on Kinect for Xbox 360. His role in the game was to bring his iconic talent and lend out his authentic choreography of his dance routines to his hit songs "Scream" and "[OMG](/wiki/OMG_(Usher_song) "OMG (Usher song)")".[[407]](#cite_note-407) Usher himself provides voiceover instruction for players participating in the Dance Central 3 game.[[408]](#cite_note-408) At the launch of the game Usher gave a surprise performance of his song "Scream".

In 2013, Usher partnered with [Samsung](/wiki/Samsung "Samsung") shooting a 2:28 minute short film directed by Rich Lee that included his single "[Looking 4 Myself](/wiki/Looking_4_Myself_(song) "Looking 4 Myself (song)")" from his then new album. The video features two Ushers in a face-off, as "down-to-earth" Usher seeks revenge against his "celebrity" alter ego after fame destroys his relationship. According to superstar, Hancock and The Matrix inspired the choreographed fight scenes with himself.[[409]](#cite_note-409) The short film featured him using a Samsung's smart TV using the new motion control features that allows users to swipe through the channels. The commercial generated twenty-two million views making it the second highest viewed Samsung commercial behind LeBron's Day with the [Samsung Galaxy Note II](/wiki/Samsung_Galaxy_Note_II "Samsung Galaxy Note II").[[410]](#cite_note-410)

In 2015, Usher teamed up with [Pepsi](/wiki/Pepsi "Pepsi") for The New Pepsi Challenge along celebrities [Serena Williams](/wiki/Serena_Williams "Serena Williams"), [Usain Bolt](/wiki/Usain_Bolt "Usain Bolt"), and many others. Every month, Pepsi "ambassadors" will use social media to issue a new challenge, many of which blend social responsibility with popular culture that encourages consumers to "do something different."[[411]](#cite_note-411) Usher's challenge invited fans to capture a single photo of an image that they feel inspires and amazes others to look at the world in a new way.[[412]](#cite_note-412) The winners photo would be selected and used in an upcoming Pepsi film.

In 2022, Usher teamed up with Rémy Martin; the company launched their first NFT's and also collectible bottles. Usher starred in television commercials to promote Rémy Martin Cognac Fine Champagne.[[413]](#cite_note-413)[[414]](#cite_note-414)

### Philanthropy

Usher founded [New Look](/wiki/Usher%27s_New_Look "Usher's New Look"), a non-profit charity organization which aims to "provide young people with a new look on life through education and real-world experience". Its flagship project, camp New Look, ran from July 11 to 23, 2005, in [Clark Atlanta University](/wiki/Clark_Atlanta_University "Clark Atlanta University").[[415]](#cite_note-415) In 2006, the charity started an initiative called Our Block, for which it helped rebuild and revitalize city blocks in New Orleans. The project went on one street at a time, and the funding was helped through part of the proceeds of Usher's team-up with [Armani Exchange](/wiki/Armani_Exchange#Armani_Exchange "Armani Exchange") in creating "Love 4 Life" dog tags, which were made available at the company's stores and Web site.[[416]](#cite_note-416) Since then, Usher has launched chapters of New Look in such cities as Baltimore, Detroit, Los Angeles, Milwaukee, New York, and Washington, D.C. New Look has four basic pillars; Talent, Education, Service, and Career. New Look program begins in the 8th grade and progresses through a student's high school and post-secondary education. Education is the center focus, Usher and his program ensures this. Students who participate in Usher's New Look achieve 100% graduation rates and all of New Look participants go on to Higher Education or Job Placement.[[417]](#cite_note-417)

In 1999, Usher participated in "Challenge for the Children", a benefit basketball game hosted by American boy band [NSYNC](/wiki/NSYNC "NSYNC"). The event, which was held on the campus of [Georgia State University](/wiki/Georgia_State_University "Georgia State University"), had raised an estimated $50,000 for several local charities.[[418]](#cite_note-418) In 2005, Usher is one among the artists who signed on for a [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina") relief concert.[[419]](#cite_note-419) He has also performed a public service announcement to promote [Do Something](/wiki/Do_Something "Do Something")'s campaign for civic engagement.[[420]](#cite_note-420)

After the devastating events of [Hurricane Katrina](/wiki/Hurricane_Katrina "Hurricane Katrina"), Usher launched Project Restart that would go on to help more than 700 families with housing and keeping their lights on. Usher also while in New Orleans would meet with students from Dillard and Xavier to aid them with cleaning up the city. "I'm here to physically do something and hopefully motivate other people to do the same," said Usher in an interview. Usher, because of his efforts and dedication to helping the city of New Orleans rebuild was presented the [key to the city](/wiki/Freedom_of_the_City "Freedom of the City") of New Orleans in 2008 from former Mayor [Ray Nagin](/wiki/Ray_Nagin "Ray Nagin").[[421]](#cite_note-421)[[422]](#cite_note-422) Also in this presentation, Usher in his honor was proclaimed his own day, Usher Day.[[423]](#cite_note-423)

In 2011, Usher was honored with the Freedom Award by the [National Civil Rights Museum](/wiki/National_Civil_Rights_Museum "National Civil Rights Museum") in [Memphis](/wiki/Memphis,_Tennessee "Memphis, Tennessee").[[424]](#cite_note-424) The Freedom Awards honor individuals who work for justice, equality, philanthropy, civil and human rights and humanitarian causes. Raymond was awarded this honor for his philanthropic work done by his New Look Foundation. Others honored with this award in the past Usher included [Oprah](/wiki/Oprah_Winfrey "Oprah Winfrey"), [Ruby Dee](/wiki/Ruby_Dee "Ruby Dee"), [Harry Belafonte](/wiki/Harry_Belafonte "Harry Belafonte"), [Cicely Tyson](/wiki/Cicely_Tyson "Cicely Tyson"), [Coretta Scott King](/wiki/Coretta_Scott_King "Coretta Scott King"), and other great figures.[[425]](#cite_note-425)

On February 12, 2015, Usher visited his former hometown [Chattanooga](/wiki/Chattanooga,_Tennessee "Chattanooga, Tennessee") making a surprise visit at Dalewood Middle School, the Orange Grove Center and the Center for the Creative Arts. Dalewood is the middle school he attended before moving to Atlanta and the school is not that far away from the road "Usher Raymond Parkway" that was presented to Usher in 2001.[[426]](#cite_note-426) Usher spoke to students at the Center for the Creative arts explaining the importance of education. His effort of giving back was to honor the legacy of his grandmother who passed early in the year. Who he called Nanny expressed to singer at a young age "whatever you want to do, I promise you can do it. We're going to look back one day, your teachers and I, and be very proud of you". At CCC, a donation of an undisclosed amount was made by the singer that would pay for new surfaces for the floors of two dance studios, a new digital lighting board and LED lights for the performing arts school's auditorium.[[427]](#cite_note-timesfreepress.com-427) Usher's second stop was Orange Grove Center, a nonprofit that serves adults and children with intellectual disabilities. There, he took part in a groundbreaking for planned improvements to the facility's track, said development director Heidi Hoffecker. The track will be named the Nancy Lackey Memorial Track to honor his grandmother.[[427]](#cite_note-timesfreepress.com-427)

In 2015, Usher teamed up with [Anthony Anderson](/wiki/Anthony_Anderson "Anthony Anderson"), [Kevin Hart](/wiki/Kevin_Hart "Kevin Hart"), [Big Sean](/wiki/Big_Sean "Big Sean"), [Chris Paul](/wiki/Chris_Paul "Chris Paul"), and more collectively donating more than $500,000 in scholarships that was given to 20 students attending [historically black colleges and universities](/wiki/Historically_black_colleges_and_universities "Historically black colleges and universities").[[428]](#cite_note-428) This is following the partnership with the [UNCF](/wiki/United_Negro_College_Fund "United Negro College Fund") in 2014 creating the Usher Raymond Scholarship Program, where last year he gave a student $100,000 to attend the [Tuskegee University](/wiki/Tuskegee_University "Tuskegee University").[[429]](#cite_note-429)

In 2016, Usher, alongside [Tyrese](/wiki/Tyrese_Gibson "Tyrese Gibson"), [Future](/wiki/Future_(rapper) "Future (rapper)"), [Ludacris](/wiki/Ludacris "Ludacris"), and [Jeezy](/wiki/Jeezy "Jeezy") donated $25,000 each to the UNCF during the 33rd annual Atlanta's Mayors Ball.[[430]](#cite_note-430)

On February 18, 2017, [Morehouse College](/wiki/Morehouse_College "Morehouse College") during the 29th annual "A Candle in the Dark Gala" honored Usher with the prestigious Candle Award for his philanthropy and contributions to music. The Candle Award honors recipients for excellence in athletics, business, education, entertainment, and other arenas.[[431]](#cite_note-431) Past recipients honored at the "Candle in the Dark" include [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder"), [Ray Charles](/wiki/Ray_Charles "Ray Charles"), [Quincy Jones](/wiki/Quincy_Jones "Quincy Jones"), [Berry Gordy](/wiki/Berry_Gordy "Berry Gordy"), and [Smokey Robinson](/wiki/Smokey_Robinson "Smokey Robinson").[[432]](#cite_note-432)

On January 31, 2018, Usher helped former NFL quarterback [Colin Kaepernick](/wiki/Colin_Kaepernick "Colin Kaepernick") complete his pledge to donate $1 million to oppressed communities. To complete the final $100,000 of his goal, Kaepernick launched the #10for10 campaign[[433]](#cite_note-433) He made 10 donations of $10,000 to 10 different charities with 10 celebrities matching him with $10,000. Usher selected the H.O.M.E. (Helping Oppressed Mothers Endure) charity in [Lithonia, Georgia](/wiki/Lithonia,_Georgia "Lithonia, Georgia") to receive a combined donation of $20,000.[[434]](#cite_note-434)

### Politics and activism

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2b/Usher_speech.jpg/250px-Usher_speech.jpg)](/wiki/File:Usher_speech.jpg)

Usher giving a speech at the [Service Nation Summit](/wiki/ServiceNation "ServiceNation") in 2008

In 2007, Usher launched his campaign in "Change in the South" in [Atlanta, Georgia](/wiki/Atlanta,_Georgia "Atlanta, Georgia") on the campus of Georgia Tech in support of then presidential hopeful [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama"). Obama asked Usher to serve as National Co-chair for his voter registration campaign "[Vote for Change](/wiki/Vote_for_Change "Vote for Change")".[[435]](#cite_note-435)[[436]](#cite_note-436) Doing more groundwork for Obama the following year Usher and [Kerry Washington](/wiki/Kerry_Washington "Kerry Washington") would lead a rally on the campus of [South Carolina State University](/wiki/South_Carolina_State_University "South Carolina State University") in [Orangeburg, S.C](/wiki/Orangeburg_County,_South_Carolina "Orangeburg County, South Carolina"). In 2008, Usher supported the [Service Nation](/wiki/Service_Nation "Service Nation") campaign, serving as the Service Nation Youth Chair.[[437]](#cite_note-437) Usher stated "As an artist, I am often recognized for the music I make and the performances I give. I'm blessed to have had the opportunity to be a part of many incredible moments. But this is the work that inspires me, empowering young people to become active in their communities".[[438]](#cite_note-438) Usher also released an exclusive song "Hush" that was influenced by social issues and voting, In the video he features a motivational Barack Obama speech. Usher performed "[Higher Ground](/wiki/Higher_Ground_(Stevie_Wonder_song) "Higher Ground (Stevie Wonder song)")" alongside [Stevie Wonder](/wiki/Stevie_Wonder "Stevie Wonder") and [Shakira](/wiki/Shakira "Shakira") at the [2009 presidential inauguration](/wiki/First_inauguration_of_Barack_Obama "First inauguration of Barack Obama") at the Lincoln Memorial and the National Mall in Washington, D.C., on January 18, 2009. He continued to endorse Obama in 2012 and supported on the 2012 reelection campaign.[[439]](#cite_note-439)

On September 13, 2014, Usher and [Bill Clinton](/wiki/Bill_Clinton "Bill Clinton") held a special campaign event for Georgia [US Senate](/wiki/United_States_Senate "United States Senate") candidate [Michelle Nunn](/wiki/Michelle_Nunn "Michelle Nunn").[[440]](#cite_note-440) Nunn was honored for her work with the Points of Light Foundation at a charity event in 2010 and has been a semiregular attendee at Clinton Foundation events since 2009. [Usher's New Look](/wiki/Usher%27s_New_Look "Usher's New Look") Foundation also awarded Clinton its Service Legacy Award in 2010.[[441]](#cite_note-441)

In October 2015, Usher partnered with the organization Sankofa which focuses on issues of social injustice that disproportionately affect the disenfranchised, the oppressed, and the underserved. On October 23, 2015, Usher and the founder of Sankofa, [Harry Belafonte](/wiki/Harry_Belafonte "Harry Belafonte"), a veteran [civil rights activist](/wiki/Civil_rights_activist "Civil rights activist"), led the conversation titled "Breaking the Chains of Social Injustice" at 92nd Street Y in Manhattan speaking about popular culture and activism.[[442]](#cite_note-442)[[443]](#cite_note-443) At this panel he introduced his music video ["Chains"](/wiki/Chains_(Usher_song) "Chains (Usher song)") featuring [Nas](/wiki/Nas "Nas") and [Bibi Bourelly](/wiki/Bibi_Bourelly "Bibi Bourelly"). The music video is geared towards facing the issues of social injustice and features images of [Trayvon Martin](/wiki/Trayvon_Martin "Trayvon Martin"), [Rekia Boyd](/wiki/Shooting_of_Rekia_Boyd "Shooting of Rekia Boyd"), [Sean Bell](/wiki/Sean_Bell_shooting_incident "Sean Bell shooting incident"), Marlon Brown, [Ramarley Graham](/wiki/Shooting_of_Ramarley_Graham "Shooting of Ramarley Graham"), [Amadou Diallo](/wiki/Shooting_of_Amadou_Diallo "Shooting of Amadou Diallo"), Caesar Cruz, and other recent victims of police violence.[[444]](#cite_note-444)[[445]](#cite_note-445)

In August 2015, he attended a major celebrity fundraiser at the Brentwood home of [Scooter Braun](/wiki/Scooter_Braun "Scooter Braun") held for presidential nominee [Hillary Clinton](/wiki/Hillary_Clinton "Hillary Clinton"). [Kanye West](/wiki/Kanye_West "Kanye West"), [Kim Kardashian](/wiki/Kim_Kardashian "Kim Kardashian"), and [Tom Hanks](/wiki/Tom_Hanks "Tom Hanks") were also among the crowd of 250 whose ticket prices were of $2,700 per person.[[446]](#cite_note-446)[[447]](#cite_note-447)

Usher was selected among a 33-member delegate for the [Presidential Committee for Arts and Humanities](/wiki/President%27s_Committee_on_the_Arts_and_Humanities "President's Committee on the Arts and Humanities") of the [United States of America](/wiki/United_States "United States") to [Cuba](/wiki/Cuba "Cuba") seeking to strengthen cultural ties between the two countries. Less than a month after the historic visit by President Barack Obama, a US government cultural mission to Cuba took place from April 18–21, 2016.[[448]](#cite_note-448) The President described his trip as extending the hand of friendship to the Cuban people, and this delegation, including some of America's most distinguished writers, artists, musicians, as well as arts and cultural leaders, will continue this historic collaboration with the Cuban people.[[449]](#cite_note-449)

## Personal life

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/a6/Rozonda_Thomas.jpg/250px-Rozonda_Thomas.jpg)](/wiki/File:Rozonda_Thomas.jpg)

[Chilli](/wiki/Rozonda_Thomas "Rozonda Thomas") on stage

In 2001, Usher began dating [TLC](/wiki/TLC_(group) "TLC (group)") member [Chilli Thomas](/wiki/Rozonda_Thomas "Rozonda Thomas"), and their relationship lasted for three years. In 2003, Usher started working on the album that would become *[Confessions](/wiki/Confessions_(Usher_album) "Confessions (Usher album)")*, which contained songs inspired by the personal lives of Usher and his collaborators. The songs about infidelity and pregnant mistresses were not inspired by Usher's private life, but by Jermaine Dupri's own personal challenges at the time.[[450]](#cite_note-:1-450) Dupri deliberately intended to create an air of mystery about Usher, and arouse fans' curiosity about how much of the infidelity story was Usher's own confession.[[450]](#cite_note-:1-450) The unintended result of this was to arouse Thomas's suspicions about Usher.[[450]](#cite_note-:1-450) They broke up in 2003.[[451]](#cite_note-451)

This was followed by a media frenzy surrounding the personal nature of *Confessions*. Many fans believed that the infidelity portrayed in "Confessions" was real, and the real reason for the split.[[11]](#cite_note-ReidKing-11) In February 2004, in an interview on *The Bert Show* on the [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta") radio channel [Q100](/wiki/WWWQ "WWWQ"), Thomas was perceived as insinuating that Usher cheated on her: "Usher did the ultimate no-no to me. ... I will never be with him again, and that is that".[[452]](#cite_note-452) Usher defended: "it just didn't work out. But cheating is not what caused the relationship to collide and crash. That ain't what broke it up".[[11]](#cite_note-ReidKing-11) This was backed up years later by Thomas, who said "People assumed that album was about us, but it was not."[[453]](#cite_note-453)

In 2004, it was reported that Usher briefly dated British supermodel [Naomi Campbell](/wiki/Naomi_Campbell "Naomi Campbell") for a few months, together the pair attended the [MTV Europe Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Europe_Music_Awards "MTV Europe Music Awards") and it was reported that Campbell jumped out of a [pop out cake](/wiki/Pop_out_cake "Pop out cake") to surprise him for his birthday.[[454]](#cite_note-454) The following year, he was linked to model Eishia Brightwell for some time. They were seen together at different events including the [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), the *[Vanity Fair](/wiki/Vanity_Fair_(magazine) "Vanity Fair (magazine)")* Oscar party and the [NAACP Awards](/wiki/NAACP_Image_Awards "NAACP Image Awards").[[455]](#cite_note-455)

In November 2005, Usher began dating stylist [Tameka Foster](/wiki/Tameka_Foster "Tameka Foster"), who worked as his personal stylist for several years.[[456]](#cite_note-people20072-456) Their engagement was announced in February 2007.[[457]](#cite_note-people20071-457) After the sudden cancellation of a planned July wedding in [the Hamptons](/wiki/The_Hamptons "The Hamptons"),[[458]](#cite_note-458)[[459]](#cite_note-459) the two were wed on August 3, 2007, in a civil ceremony in [Atlanta](/wiki/Atlanta "Atlanta").[[460]](#cite_note-460) This was followed by a larger wedding ceremony on September 1, 2007, held at the Chateau Elan Winery & Resort outside Atlanta.[[461]](#cite_note-461)

Usher's father, Usher Raymond III, died on January 21, 2008.[[462]](#cite_note-462)

Following his marriage to Foster, they had two sons together: Usher "Cinco" Raymond V, born in November 2007,[[463]](#cite_note-463) and Naviyd Ely Raymond, born in December 2008.[[464]](#cite_note-464)

In February 2009, two months after the birth of their second son, Foster suffered a [cardiac arrest](/wiki/Cardiac_arrest "Cardiac arrest") in [São Paulo](/wiki/S%C3%A3o_Paulo "São Paulo"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil"), when she was given [general anesthesia](/wiki/General_anesthesia "General anesthesia") prior to scheduled cosmetic surgery (reportedly [liposuction](/wiki/Liposuction "Liposuction")), which was ultimately not performed.[[465]](#cite_note-people2009-465) She was [induced into a coma](/wiki/Induced_coma "Induced coma") to aid her recovery and was transferred to a larger facility, the [Hospital Sírio-Libanês](/wiki/Hospital_S%C3%ADrio-Liban%C3%AAs "Hospital Sírio-Libanês").[[465]](#cite_note-people2009-465) Usher canceled his performance at [Clive Davis](/wiki/Clive_Davis "Clive Davis")'s pre-[Grammy](/wiki/Grammies "Grammies") Gala in order to fly to Brazil to be with Foster.[[465]](#cite_note-people2009-465) After a week of recovery, Foster's surgeon issued a statement saying that she was "doing very well".[[465]](#cite_note-people2009-465)

Usher filed for divorce from Foster in June 2009 in Atlanta.[[466]](#cite_note-466) The divorce was legally finalized in Fulton County Court on November 4, 2009.[[467]](#cite_note-467) This preceded a highly publicized [child custody](/wiki/Child_custody "Child custody") dispute spanning three years, which ultimately culminated on August 24, 2012, with a judge awarding Usher primary custody of his two sons with Foster.[[468]](#cite_note-468)

In 2011, *[Forbes](/wiki/Forbes "Forbes")* magazine placed Usher at number 32 on its [Celebrity 100](/wiki/Celebrity_100 "Celebrity 100"),[[469]](#cite_note-469) and reported his earnings after the [OMG Tour](/wiki/OMG_Tour "OMG Tour") to stand at $46 million, ranking him at number 12 on "The World's 25 Highest-Paid Musicians" list.[[470]](#cite_note-470)

Usher was reported to have adopted a [vegan](/wiki/Vegan "Vegan") diet in 2012.[[471]](#cite_note-471)

On August 5, 2013, his eldest son, Usher V, was hospitalized in the [Intensive Care Unit](/wiki/Intensive_Care_Unit "Intensive Care Unit") (ICU) after he became stuck in a drain while swimming in a pool at Usher's home.[[472]](#cite_note-472) The next day, Foster filed a request for an emergency custody hearing in Fulton County Superior Court, seeking temporary primary custody and citing Usher V's "near-death accident" as evidence of a lack of supervision of the children.[[473]](#cite_note-473) At the hearing on August 9, 2013, the case was dismissed and Usher retained primary custody of their two sons.[[474]](#cite_note-474)

In September 2015, Usher quietly married his long-time girlfriend and manager Grace Miguel while vacationing in [Cuba](/wiki/Cuba "Cuba"). There was speculation of the two being married when Miguel's Instagram post showed a picture of a gold wedding band on Usher's finger. He confirmed the marriage during an interview on *[The Ellen DeGeneres Show](/wiki/The_Ellen_DeGeneres_Show "The Ellen DeGeneres Show")*.[[475]](#cite_note-475)[[476]](#cite_note-476)

Three claimants sued Usher on allegations of fraud, sexual battery, infliction of emotional distress and more, alleging that he was positive for the [herpes](/wiki/Herpes "Herpes") virus and did not tell them in advance. Each were represented by attorney Lisa Bloom, who said on August 8, 2017, that one of her three clients had contracted genital herpes allegedly from him. One of the accusers, Quantasia Sharpton, said she had unprotected sex with him after a concert she attended. The other two accusers, one male and one female, preferred to remain anonymous plaintiffs.[[477]](#cite_note-latimes1502134431-477) Sharpton admitted she tested negative and Bloom stated she did not know if the singer had the disease.[[478]](#cite_note-478)

On March 6, 2018, Usher and wife Grace Miguel informed *[Us Weekly](/wiki/Us_Weekly "Us Weekly")* of their separation in a statement that read, in part: "After much thought and consideration we have mutually decided to separate as a couple ... We remain deeply connected, loving friends who will continue supporting each other through the next phases of our lives".[[479]](#cite_note-479) On December 28, 2018, Usher filed for divorce from Miguel in Georgia.[[480]](#cite_note-480)

In September 2020, it was confirmed that Usher was expecting his third child, with music executive Jenn Goicoechea, whom he reportedly started dating in October 2019.[[481]](#cite_note-481)[[482]](#cite_note-482) Their daughter was born on September 24, 2020.[[483]](#cite_note-483) Usher and Goicoecha welcomed their second child together on September 29, 2021.[[484]](#cite_note-484) Usher and Goiciechea married in [Las Vegas, Nevada](/wiki/Las_Vegas,_Nevada "Las Vegas, Nevada") on February 11, 2024, immediately following Usher's Super Bowl performance.[[485]](#cite_note-485)[[486]](#cite_note-486)

## Discography

Main article: [Usher discography](/wiki/Usher_discography "Usher discography")

**Solo studio albums**
